# Etherstack

> Tactical radio and mission-critical communications technology; UK-incorporated, ASX-listed.

## What they do

Etherstack plc is a wireless communications technology company that designs, develops and deploys radio products for defence, public safety and utility networks. It specialises in waveform software, radio protocol stacks and deployable tactical communications systems, supplying defence organisations, public-safety agencies, carriers and utilities. The UK operating entity is Etherstack Wireless Limited; the parent is incorporated in the UK and listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: ESK), with R&D across the UK, Australia, the USA and Japan.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence waveforms** - P25, TETRA, DMR and NXDN protocol stacks plus NATO and sovereign waveforms, ported across multiple radio platforms.
- **Tactical communications** - deployable repeater and push-to-talk-over-IP systems for resilient field communications.
- **LMR-IWF** - 3GPP-compliant interworking function for migrating land mobile radio to LTE and 5G.
- **Cryptography and key management** - AES-256 encryption for secure voice and data.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# Ethicronics

> Cambridge deep-tech startup developing software to verify the integrity and authenticity of electronic hardware components across defence and critical supply chains.

## What they do

Ethicronics develops a software-as-a-service platform to establish trust in electronics hardware, addressing the risk of counterfeit, tampered, cloned or maliciously modified components. Its solution generates hardware integrity certificates by applying unforgeable digital stamps at PCB to nanometer-level resolution, enabling supply chain assurance for defence, aerospace, space, transport, finance and critical infrastructure. The company was incorporated in 2022 and is based at the Allia Future Business Centre, Cambridge.

## Products and solutions

- **Hardware Integrity SaaS platform** - a scalable software solution that certifies and authenticates electronic components, producing hardware integrity certificates for defence and critical-sector supply chains.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2024-08** - Ethicronics received Defence Technology Exploitation Programme (DTEP) funding from MOD/DASA to develop its hardware assurance solution in partnership with Thales as the higher-tier supplier. [source](https://www.gov.uk/government/news/dtep-funding-propels-two-additional-small-businesses)

companies.summary: UK network of test and compliance laboratories providing military EMC, environmental simulation and product certification to defence, aerospace and commercial customers.
companies.body:
# Eurofins E&E UK

> UK network of test and compliance laboratories providing military EMC, environmental simulation and product certification to defence, aerospace and commercial customers.

## What they do

Eurofins E&E UK is an end-to-end testing, certification and compliance provider covering electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), electrical safety, environmental simulation and radio equipment testing. Its UK network of UKAS-accredited laboratories, headquartered in York, serves military and commercial product programmes across land, sea and air domains. The network includes the Eurofins York brand (formerly York EMC Services) and laboratory sites in Devon, Southampton, London, Bristol, Castleford and Grangemouth. For projects requiring security clearance, the York site handles those enquiries. Eurofins E&E UK is part of the Eurofins Scientific group, headquartered in Luxembourg.

## Products and solutions

- **Military EMC testing** - UKAS-accredited testing to DEF STAN 59-411 and MIL-STD-461 (variants D through G) for land, sea and air platforms.
- **Environmental simulation** - extreme environmental testing (vibration, shock, temperature, humidity, ingress) for military and defence applications.
- **EMC training** - a four-day EMC for Military course covering DEF STAN 59-411, MIL-STD-461 and related standards.
- **Fibre optic testing** - testing to MIL-PRF-28876, MIL-PRF-85045 and MIL-DLT-83526.
- **Electromagnetic shielding effectiveness** - testing for materials and enclosures up to 40 GHz, including comb generator emitters.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** UKAS accreditation covering DEF STAN 59-411, DEF STAN 59-41, MIL-STD-461.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.eurofins.co.uk/electrical-and-electronics/
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/eurofins-e-e-uk

## Sources

Eurofins E&E UK is part of Eurofins Scientific SE, listed on Euronext Brussels, with global HQ in Luxembourg.

companies.name: Eutelsat OneWeb
companies.summary: Integrated GEO-LEO satellite operator providing government and defence connectivity across land, maritime, and air domains.
companies.body:
# Eutelsat OneWeb

> Integrated GEO-LEO satellite operator providing government and defence connectivity across land, maritime, and air domains.

## What they do

Eutelsat OneWeb was formed in September 2023 through the merger of Eutelsat Communications and OneWeb, combining Eutelsat's 36 geostationary (GEO) satellites with OneWeb's 652-satellite low-earth-orbit (LEO) constellation operating at approximately 1,200 km altitude. The company provides hybrid multi-orbit satellite broadband connectivity across commercial, government, and defence verticals. The UK Government holds a golden share and a board seat in the company. The parent group, Eutelsat Communications SA, is headquartered in Paris; the UK operational centre is at West Works Building, 195 Wood Lane, London.

## Products and solutions

- **OneWeb LEO constellation** - 652-satellite LEO network providing low-latency broadband at global coverage, used for government and military connectivity including Comms-on-the-Move applications.
- **Intellian OW7MP manpack terminal** - the first military-grade LEO manpack terminal for the OneWeb network, co-developed with Intellian; fits a standard military rucksack, supports Comms-on-the-Move (5-130 kph) and Comms-on-the-Pause, and operates in GPS-denied environments with throughput up to 57 x 10 Mbps.
- **Kymeta Goshawk u8 flat-panel terminal** - multi-orbit terminal available on the Eutelsat OneWeb LEO network for government and military customers.
- **Satellite Virtual Network Operator (SVNO)** - digital product giving government and defence customers full control over SATCOM service provisioning.
- **Hybrid multi-orbit SATCOM** - combined GEO high-throughput and LEO low-latency configuration supporting ISR missions across manned and unmanned aircraft, land, and maritime platforms.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; UKspace

## Recent news

- **2026-06-15** - Signed the CENTAURE contract, the first call-off under the EUR 1 billion NEXUS framework agreement with the French Ministry of the Armed Forces; total value approximately EUR 350 million over up to eight years for LEO capacity for French armed forces. [Satellite Today](https://www.satellitetoday.com/connectivity/2026/06/16/eutelsat-secures-first-order-under-french-defense-agreement/)
- **2026-02** - Launched the Intellian OW7MP, the first military-grade manpack terminal for the OneWeb LEO network. [Mynewsdesk](https://www.mynewsdesk.com/eutelsat/pressreleases/eutelsat-and-intellian-introduce-first-leo-manpack-terminal-for-government-and-defence-connectivity-3432227)
- **2026-01** - Airbus Defence and Space awarded a contract for 340 next-generation OneWeb LEO satellites, valued at approximately EUR 2 billion, with deliveries beginning late 2026. [Airbus](https://www.airbus.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2026-01-airbus-awarded-eutelsat-contract-for-further-340-low-earth-orbit)
- **2025-07** - FCDO Services signed a LEO capacity deal via NSSL Global for British embassies, high commissions, and consulates, including for defence and policing operations; the UK also invested EUR 90 million into Eutelsat's capital raise. [Satellite Today](https://www.satellitetoday.com/government-military/2025/07/21/uk-governments-fcdo-services-signs-deal-for-eutelsat-oneweb-leo-services/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.eutelsat.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/eutelsatoneweb

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (eutelsat.com), ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/eutelsat-oneweb-1/), UKspace member directory (ukspace.org/member/eutelsat-oneweb/), Companies House (12534512 - OneWeb Holdings Limited; 04830990 - Eutelsat UK Limited), Satellite Today, Airbus newsroom, Mynewsdesk press releases.

companies.body:
# Eviden

> Atos Group brand for cybersecurity, vision AI and mission-critical systems, with a defence offering serving European and Five Eyes ministries of defence.

## What they do

Eviden is the Atos Group brand for advanced computing, cybersecurity products, mission-critical systems and vision AI. It builds cyber-secure-by-design systems and electronics, data platforms for equipment availability and personnel readiness, and high-performance and AI computing, positioning around digital sovereignty and mission-critical applications. It runs a defence offering under its Mission-Critical Systems unit and operates a Cyber Defence Centre providing offensive security, red teaming and penetration testing. Eviden is part of Atos SE, which is publicly listed on Euronext Paris.

## Products and solutions

- **Cybersecurity products** - data encryption, digital identity management, post-quantum security, and a Cyber Defence Centre offering penetration testing, red teaming and phishing simulation.
- **Mission-critical systems** - defence, public safety and transport systems, including secure real-time communications.
- **Vision AI** - including ELIT AI, a radar-signals sensor with built-in sovereign AI.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

# Evolve Dynamics

> British designer and manufacturer of all-weather, long-endurance unmanned air systems.

## What they do

Evolve Dynamics is a UK engineering company that designs, develops and manufactures unmanned air systems, payloads and software. Its systems are built for extreme-weather, persistent operation and are used across defence, police and security, search and rescue, and infrastructure inspection. companies.body:
# EW Solutions Ltd

> Electronic warfare, CEMA, and OSINT consultancy and intelligence products company serving defence organisations and government agencies.

## What they do

EW Solutions Ltd (trading as EWS) is a security-cleared SME founded in 2009, specialising in electronic warfare, Cyber and Electromagnetic Activities (CEMA), and OSINT-driven intelligence. The company provides vendor-agnostic consultancy, proprietary open-source intelligence products, and EW training and engineering support. Customers include armed forces, national agencies, government bodies, and defence industry OEMs. Registered and operational at Long Bennington Business Park, Nottinghamshire.

## Products and solutions

- **OSTD (Open Source Threat Database)** - unclassified, daily-updated global repository of IED and RCIED events; over 40,000 entries; used by national agencies for threat intelligence.
- **OverSite** - UxV threat database tracking UAV, UGV, USV, and UUV events, tactics, techniques and procedures, and ECM development data; available standalone or alongside OSTD.
- **Country Reports and RFI service** - tailored regional threat assessments with multilingual analysis.
- **EW and CEMA consultancy** - capability development, procurement support, red team assessments, bid support, market and competitor analysis, and technology integration for OEMs seeking routes to market.
- **EW and CEMA training** - covers JSP822 Training Needs Analysis, CEMA doctrine development, and EOD support; delivered in the UK and in deployed environments.
- **Engineering and Field Service** - technical support and Field Service Representatives (FSRs) for EW system lifecycle support.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Counter-EO UK (ADS Special Interest Group)

## Recent news

- **2026-05-05** - Published article: "How Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) is Shaping Modern Security Operations". [EWS website](https://www.solutions-ew.com)
- **2026-03-16** - Published articles on CEMA consultancy services and OSINT training for defence personnel. [EWS website](https://www.solutions-ew.com)

## Links

- Website: https://www.solutions-ew.com

companies.summary: Electronic warfare simulation hardware manufacturer producing multi-spectral threat and countermeasure simulators for test, evaluation, and training.
companies.body:
# EWST Ltd

> Electronic warfare simulation hardware manufacturer producing multi-spectral threat and countermeasure simulators for test, evaluation, and training.

## What they do

EWST Ltd (Electronic Warfare Simulation Technologies) designs and manufactures multi-spectral threat and countermeasure simulators used for EW test, evaluation, and training by naval, air force, army, and civilian defence organisations across 30 countries. The company has over 40 years of product history, operating previously as EW Simulation Technology Ltd under Ultra Electronics before being carved out of the Ultra I&C (Cobham) portfolio. It was registered as an independent company in April 2023 under the name Ultra EWST Limited, then renamed EWST Ltd in May 2025 following a change of ownership to EWST Holdings Ltd. Over 100 simulation systems have been delivered since 1984. The operational base is Building A8, Cody Technology Park, Farnborough, Hampshire.

## Products and solutions

- **Chameleon** - radar target generator and ECM signal generator; multi-channel, multi-DRFM architecture; 500 MHz to 40 GHz coverage; supports hardware-in-the-loop and radiating applications.
- **MERTS (Mobile EW and Radar Test System)** - fully mobile system integrating ECM (Chameleon) and radar threat generation; includes antenna azimuth/elevation positioner with TV camera and GPS tracking.
- **PRS (Portable Radar Simulator)** - portable radar simulation system.
- **RSS8000** - radar threat simulation system.
- **EMES9000** - high-fidelity electromagnetic spectrum simulation system.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://ewst.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/electronic-warfare-simulation-technologies

companies.body:
# Exact CNC (NI) Ltd

> Northern Ireland precision CNC machining and finishing company supplying the aerospace, space and defence supply chain.

## What they do

Exact CNC (NI) Ltd is a precision engineering subcontractor based in Newry, operating primarily in the aerospace, space and defence sectors. From a 72,000 sq ft facility at Craigmore Mill Industrial Estate, the company provides CNC milling and turning across 2D and 3D configurations from 3-axis to 9-axis, covering composites, plastics, tooling board and metals. It also offers anodising, chemical coating, powder coating, CMM inspection and 3D scanning, and metal fabrication and assembly. Named customers include Collins Aerospace, Thales UK and Thompson Aero Seating.

## Products and solutions

- **Precision CNC milling and turning** - multi-axis machining (3- to 9-axis) of aerospace and defence components in metal and composite materials.
- **Cerakote ceramic coating** - ceramic-polymer finishing used on military small arms, weapon accessories and tactical equipment; Exact Group is a certified applicator.
- **Surface treatment and finishing** - anodising, chemical coating and powder coating to aerospace supply chain standards.
- **CMM inspection and 3D scanning** - dimensional verification and quality control against AS9100 Rev D requirements.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS9100 Rev D; ISO 9001; ISO 13485; SC21 Silver (first company in Ireland to achieve Silver on first engagement; reaccredited July 2024 for the fourth consecutive year); SC21 Operational Excellence; EFQM 5 Star (2023); Cyber Essentials Plus (achieved June 2026).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

- **2025-04-02** - Exhibited at Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) in Hamburg on the Invest NI stand. [source](https://www.adsadvance.co.uk/ni-companies-to-highlight-region-s-aerospace-and-supply-chain-at-aix.html)

companies.name: Exail Ltd
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of Exail Technologies SA, a France-headquartered group supplying autonomous mine countermeasure systems, unmanned maritime vehicles and inertial navigation systems to navies and defence forces worldwide.
companies.body:
# Exail Ltd

> UK subsidiary of Exail Technologies SA, a France-headquartered group supplying autonomous mine countermeasure systems, unmanned maritime vehicles and inertial navigation systems to navies and defence forces worldwide.

## What they do

Exail Ltd is the UK entity of Exail Technologies SA, listed on Euronext Paris and formed in 2022 from the merger of ECA Group (drone and robotics) and iXblue (navigation and photonics). The group employs approximately 2,000 people globally and operates in over 80 countries, with the UK registered office in Petersfield, Hampshire, and operational offices in Aberdeen and Wokingham. The company's defence focus is on autonomous mine countermeasures, unmanned maritime vehicles, and fibre optic gyroscope-based inertial navigation systems, with over 70 navies and armies among its customers. The UK entity serves the Royal Navy and allied defence customers and is actively engaged in UK MCM programme work.

## Products and solutions

- **UMIS (Unmanned MCM Integrated System)** - a fully unmanned mine countermeasure system integrating autonomous surface, underwater and aerial vehicles for end-to-end MCM missions.
- **A18-M AUV** - mid-size autonomous underwater vehicle for long-endurance MCM operations; up to 24 hours endurance, covering 20-40 sq km per mission.
- **A9-M AUV** - smaller autonomous underwater vehicle for MCM tasks.
- **DriX USV** - unmanned surface vessel series used for hydrographic survey, surveillance and mine countermeasures; in service in 19 countries.
- **Inertial navigation systems (INS)** - fibre optic gyroscope-based navigation and motion sensing for ships, submarines, UUVs, UAVs and land platforms.
- **Photonics and fibre optic components** - optical components and laser systems for space, telecoms and scientific applications (legacy iXblue capability).

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2026-04** - Exail and JFD signed a pre-bid agreement to collaborate on UK-based maintenance, training and support services for Royal Navy mine countermeasures, positioning ahead of the Royal Navy's transition to a hybrid manned/unmanned MCM force. [source](https://www.navalnews.com/naval-news/2026/04/exail-and-jfd-partner-to-increase-resilience-in-future-uk-mcm-capability/)
- **2025-09** - Exhibited at DSEI 2025 (London, stand S8-142), showcasing navigation, mine countermeasures and environmental awareness solutions. [source](https://www.exail.com/events/dsei-2025)

## Links

- Website: https://www.exail.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/exail

companies.body:
# Excelerate Technology Ltd

> Welsh systems integrator providing hybrid satellite and cellular communications, deployable connectivity and situational awareness solutions to military, government and emergency services customers.

## What they do

Excelerate Technology Ltd is a Cardiff-based communications and situational awareness systems integrator with over 20 years of experience serving military, first responder and government markets. The company designs and supplies hybrid connectivity systems that bond satellite, 4G and 5G links to provide resilient, on-the-move communications independent of terrestrial network availability. Excelerate positions itself as a niche and agnostic integrator, supporting deployable command and control, vehicle-mounted and portable communications platforms. Defence is an explicit named vertical on their public website.

## Products and solutions

- **Hybrid communications systems** - vehicle-mounted and portable platforms bonding satellite, 4G and 5G for high-throughput, resilient military connectivity.
- **Hybrid Edge Appliance** - 4G/5G/satellite bonding device for deployable private secure networks.
- **Linx Hub** - portable Wi-Fi hotspot for field connectivity.
- **Video streaming and situational awareness** - live video and data solutions for command and control in deployed environments.
- **Deployable command support units** - communications-equipped command vehicles and incident command units, built to customer specification for emergency services and military end-users.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001 (held since September 2008); Cyber Essentials Plus.

## Recent news

companies.name: EXFO Europe Ltd
companies.summary: UK and European subsidiary of EXFO Inc, a Canadian test and measurement company with a dedicated defence and homeland security division covering fibre optic network testing, tactical communications intelligence and counter-UAV systems.
companies.body:
# EXFO Europe Ltd

> UK and European subsidiary of EXFO Inc, a Canadian test and measurement company with a dedicated defence and homeland security division covering fibre optic network testing, tactical communications intelligence and counter-UAV systems.

## What they do

EXFO Europe Ltd is the UK-registered subsidiary of EXFO Inc, headquartered in Quebec City, Canada, with approximately 1,500 employees globally. The UK entity, based in Chandler's Ford, Eastleigh, operates across two distinct capability areas: fibre optic and network test and measurement equipment serving defence laboratories and supply chains, and a dedicated Homeland Security Division offering tactical communications intelligence systems. EXFO's defence test and measurement products are used to verify fibre connectivity from the lab to live deployed environments across all branches of armed forces; the Homeland Security Division products serve elite police forces, military special forces and government security agencies. The company exhibited at Security and Policing 2026, the NPSA's closed government-facing exhibition at Farnborough.

## Products and solutions

- **Fibre optic test and measurement** - OTDRs, fibre inspection scopes, field certifiers and lab instruments for verifying fibre infrastructure in defence and aerospace environments; includes the FIP-500 fibre inspection probe and MaxTester 945 Fiber Certifier.
- **5G-to-the-battlefield network testing** - tools for validating mobile network performance in deployed military environments.
- **IMSI catchers with direction-finding** - portable, vehicular, fixed-site and airborne systems for cellular interception and geolocation.
- **Mobile device interrogation and network analysis** - equipment for mobile phone interrogation, cellular and WiFi interception, and mobile service blocking.
- **Counter-UAV measures** - systems described in the Homeland Security portfolio targeting small UAS threats.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2008 and TL9000 (corporate level); EXFO Certified Contractor programme (BICSI-recognised, 2,000+ registered contractors).
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2026-02** - Announced the first native 24-fibre testers for data centre validation. [source](https://www.exfo.com/en/corporate/news-events/)
- **2026** - Exhibited at Security and Policing 2026 at Farnborough, confirming active engagement with UK government and law enforcement. [source](https://www.securityandpolicing.co.uk/exhibitors/exhibitors-list-2026/exfo-europe/)
- **2025-06** - Vodafone Germany licensed EXFO Context, a knowledge-graph semantic modelling tool for network visibility. [source](https://www.exfo.com/en/corporate/news-events/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.exfo.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/exfo

EXFO company website (exfo.com/en/solutions/government), Companies House (02316168), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/exfo-europe-ltd-2), Security and Policing 2026 exhibitor listing, PR Newswire (Alker Fibre Optic Specialists partnership, May 2023).

companies.name: Exotopic Ltd
companies.summary: London-based space and defence accelerator and mission-delivery company, running the UK Space Agency Accelerator and delivering in-orbit demonstration missions for defence-relevant SMEs.
companies.body:
# Exotopic Ltd

> London-based space and defence accelerator and mission-delivery company, running the UK Space Agency Accelerator and delivering in-orbit demonstration missions for defence-relevant SMEs.

## What they do

Exotopic Ltd is a London SME operating at the intersection of space mission delivery, investment and defence-sector accelerator management. The company is the lead partner delivering the UK Space Agency Accelerator programme and has won contracts with both the European Space Agency and the Defence and Security Accelerator (DASA). Its primary commercial offering is an in-orbit demonstration (IOD) service, "Give Me Some Space!", which provides defence and aerospace SMEs with affordable access to space to test and validate spacecraft technologies. Exotopic is also a technical advisory business, supporting companies with investment readiness and business development in the defence and space sectors. The company is registered at the British Interplanetary Society building in Vauxhall, London.

## Products and solutions

- **Give Me Some Space! IOD missions** - rideshare in-orbit demonstration service giving SMEs access to low-Earth orbit to test propulsion, sensors, computing and other spacecraft payloads; first mission targeted Q1 2026, second (targeting Q1 2027) open to European and international entrants.
- **UK Space Agency Accelerator** - Exotopic delivers this programme as lead partner, supporting early-stage space and defence-adjacent companies.
- **SAC Technical Expert Support (TES)** - free access to technical expert advice for SMEs, delivered as part of Exotopic's accelerator remit.
- **MachLab** - a propulsion test and research facility supporting rocket engine testing up to 10 kN across a range of technology readiness levels.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; UKspace (SME member).

## Recent news

- **2026** - Announced the payload manifest for the debut "Give Me Some Space!" IOD mission, with passengers including Stars Edge (VLEO thruster) and Lodestar (TESSERACT edge computing and sensors for on-orbit robotic capture). [source](https://www.ukspace.org/exotopic-announces-manifest-for-breakthrough-uk-satellite-mission/)
- **2025** - Announced a second IOD mission targeting Q1 2027 due to demand, with MagDrive confirmed as first passenger; open to European and international SMEs. [source](https://www.exotopic.com/give-me-some-space-mission)
- **2025** - TechUK feature on Exotopic's "Tickets to Orbit" approach and its impact on UK tech scale-ups accessing space. [source](https://www.techuk.org/resource/bringing-space-to-uk-tech-scale-ups-how-exotopic-s-tickets-to-orbit-are-accelerating-time-to-market.html)

## Links

- Website: https://www.exotopic.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/exotopic

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (exotopic.com), Companies House (09238982), ADS Group members directory, UKspace SME member listing, TechUK, UK Space Agency/UKspace IOD mission announcement.

# Expando AB

> Swedish specialist in rugged COTS computing, embedded systems, and space and avionics components for defence and aerospace.

## What they do

Expando AB designs, integrates, and supplies rugged computing hardware and specialist electronic components to defence, aerospace, and space customers. Founded in 2000 and owned by the Tisenhult Group, the company operates from Sollentuna, north of Stockholm, and serves Nordic and European prime contractors. Its work spans naval platforms, missile systems, surveillance systems, and satellite applications. Named customers include Kongsberg, SAAB, BAE Systems, and Patria.

## Products and solutions

- **Rugged servers and board computers** - mission computers and small-form-factor tactical systems for naval, missile, and surveillance platforms.
- **High-performance embedded computing (HPEC)** - SIGINT, EW, mission computing, and C5ISR applications, including collaboration with EIZO Rugged Solutions and NVIDIA Jetson.
- **Space and avionics components** - radiation-hardened power supply, memory, computer cores, and space cameras; ARINC components (429, 571, 575).
- **Custom ASIC and bespoke semiconductor solutions** - flight-critical software and power supply subsystems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# Explosive Learning Solutions

> Defence and government training organisation specialising in training needs analysis, e-learning development, end-point assessment, and ordnance, munitions and explosives education.

## What they do

Explosive Learning Solutions (ELS) is an independent learning and development (L&D) accredited training organisation, consultancy company, and End Point Assessment Organisation (EPAO). Since 2005 it has delivered Training Needs Analysis, training design, digital and blended e-learning development, and training delivery to the UK MOD (Defence Academy and Defence Equipment and Support), UK National Government, and major defence organisations. ELS applies the Defence Systems Approach to Training (DSAT) across its work and is a formally recognised Vocational Education and Training (VET) provider, as well as a company member of the Institute of Explosive Engineers (IExpE) supporting Ordnance, Munitions and Explosives (OME) contracts.

## Products and solutions

- **Training Needs Analysis and design** - bespoke TNA, training design, and digital content for blended, virtual, and e-learning programmes aligned to DSAT best practice.
- **End Point Assessment (EPA)** - multi-year EPA contracts across defence, aviation, supply chain, NDT, and data disciplines at Levels 2 through 6, including OME Technician and Intelligence Analyst standards.
- **P3M and procurement training** - commercial off-the-shelf and bespoke training in Projects, Programmes and Portfolios methodologies and defence acquisition skills, delivered at Harwell Science and Innovation Campus or at customer sites.
- **CBRN and counter-terror consultancy** - specialist advisory and training services in CBRN, covert and counter-terror, hostile environment, and bomb disposal domains.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR registered
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001, Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus, SC21 Bronze
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Institute of Explosive Engineers (IExpE); approved training organisation for City and Guilds, ILM, APMG International, PeopleCert, APM, Skills for Justice, and CIPS

## Links

- Website: https://www.elsbusinesstraining.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/explosive-learning-solutions-ltd

companies.body:
# Exsel Electronics

> Software-centric systems integrator focused on data-centric security and multi-domain information management for defence and government.

## What they do

Exsel Electronics is a division of the Exsel Group, operating as a standalone legal entity based in Welshpool, Powys. It is a software-centric systems integrator with a specialised focus on information and cyber security, working closely with governments and defence organisations to pioneer interoperable information security systems. Its core domain covers multi-domain communications, data, and system management, with an emphasis on ensuring users have continuous security and accessibility of information across all domains. The approach aligns with the UK National Cyber Security Strategy.

## Products and solutions

- **Need2Know** - a document-level data protection product that safeguards secret, sensitive, or personal information contained within documents before they are shared, providing a single source of truth for information control.
- **Data Centric Security** - a unified security model for governments and organisations that protects data and ensures business continuity across hybrid work environments, cloud computing, and complex regulatory frameworks.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group (membership held by parent Exsel Group Ltd)

# Exsel Group

> Independent defence technology group providing engineering design and integration, electronics, training and simulation, ballistic protection, and field services to defence and industry.

## What they do

The Exsel Group is an independent defence technology solutions company delivering engineering solutions, technology systems, and support services to defence and industry where reliability, safety, and protection are paramount. Originating in 1947, the group now operates across three UK sites and serves a global customer base spanning government, defence, and commercial interests. Exsel Group is the official sole representative and approved agent for ND Defense's complete range of vehicles and parts across the UK, Europe, and West Africa.

## Products and solutions

- **Design and integration** - electromechanical, electronic, and communication systems integration into vehicle and mobile platforms; container-based solutions for rapid deployment and severe environment applications; build-to-print and full design-and-development programmes.
- **Electronics** - software and electronic engineering for defence systems including obsolescence management, through-life support, repair and maintenance, and data-centric security (delivered through the Exsel Electronics division).
- **Training and simulation** - design, production, assembly, and installation of training and simulation hardware devices for high-fidelity representative training environments.
- **Dyna Armour** - ballistic and blast protection solutions for personnel, platforms, and assets, designed and developed in partnership with Dynasystems.
- **Field services** - global maintenance, servicing, and parts supply for defence vehicle fleets including ND Defense legacy and current vehicles.

companies.body:
# ExTrac

> British AI-driven open-source intelligence and geopolitical risk-analytics company serving defence, security and government.

## What they do

ExTrac is a British OSINT and geopolitical risk-intelligence company. It combines curated data, human subject-matter expertise and domain-specific AI to detect and forecast risk across the physical world, online, and where the two intersect. It ingests high-relevance, hard-to-reach data streams to track violence, map narratives and surface emerging threats, supporting customers in defence, security, government, public safety, executive protection and risk monitoring. The registered entity is ExTrac AI Limited.

## Products and solutions

- **ExTrac platform** - a customisable web application for leaders, decision-makers and analysts, hiding complexity by default while keeping the underlying data fully accessible and auditable.
- **Co-Analyst** - a domain-specific, agentic AI that retrieves data, surfaces insight and generates visualisations and reports in response to analyst requests.
- **Curated data feeds and bespoke reporting** - expert-curated, hard-to-access data streams, with the option for customers to integrate their own data, plus a mobile app and bespoke reporting.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** listed on the UK G-Cloud Digital Marketplace as a supplier.
- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

companies.body:
# ExTrac AI

> British OSINT company providing an AI-powered intelligence platform for defence and security customers tracking conflict, grey zone, and influence activities.

## What they do

ExTrac AI Limited is a British Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) company serving allied nations. It provides both an AI SaaS platform and bespoke intelligence reporting, sourcing, cleaning, and structuring hard-to-access adversary data to deliver expert insight at machine speed. The platform finds, curates, and ingests high-relevance data sources including communications and kinetic behaviours of hostile state and non-state actors, mining them for real-time insights on conflict, grey zone, and influence activities using AI that is both secure and compliant by design. Defence and security applications include force protection, situational awareness, operational planning, and Information Environment and Battle Damage Assessment.

## Products and solutions

- **ExTrac platform (web and mobile)** - AI SaaS platform combining curated OSINT data, domain-specific AI, and human expertise; accessible via web app, mobile app, and API, with configurable dashboards, alerts, and reporting.
- **Co-Analyst** - domain-specific agentic AI built into the ExTrac platform that retrieves, analyses, and contextualises data on instruction, producing visualisations and reports.
- **Bespoke intelligence reporting** - customer-specific reporting products drawing on ExTrac's curated data combined with client data for tailored analytical output.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR registered
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.extrac.ai
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/extracrisk

companies.name: Extreme Cases
companies.summary: UK manufacturer of bespoke rugged protective cases, containers, and backpacks for defence, military, and specialist field use.
companies.body:
# Extreme Cases

> UK manufacturer of bespoke rugged protective cases, containers, and backpacks for defence, military, and specialist field use.

## What they do

Extreme Cases designs and manufactures custom protective cases and carrying solutions for sensitive equipment transported and operated in demanding environments. The company supplies directly to defence customers including the UK MoD, Bundeswehr, NATO, and European Special Forces. Based in Barnstaple, North Devon, it holds an exclusive European distribution agreement with Zero Manufacturing, a US specialist case brand.

## Products and solutions

- **Bespoke protective cases** - custom-designed rigid cases for sensitive equipment, built to withstand transit and field conditions.
- **Rugged containers** - heavy-duty storage and transport containers for military use.
- **Field backpacks** - specialist carrying solutions for operational use.
- **Zero Manufacturing distribution** - exclusive European distributor for the Zero Manufacturing range of protective cases.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.extreme-cases.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/extreme-cases-ltd

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (extreme-cases.com); ADS Group members directory; Companies House (06957356).

companies.name: F.C. Lane Electronics Limited
companies.summary: West Sussex specialist distributor and value-added assembler of military-grade electrical connectors and interconnect solutions.
companies.body:
# F C Lane Electronics

> West Sussex specialist distributor and value-added assembler of military-grade electrical connectors and interconnect solutions.

## What they do

F C Lane Electronics is a franchised value-added distributor of military and aerospace-specification electrical connectors, established in 1966 and operating from Slinfold in West Sussex. The company holds in-house assembly and testing approval for MIL-DTL-38999 Series connectors under a QPL Category C authorisation from Eaton/Souriau, and its sister company Weald Electronics manufactures connectors to military standards on the same site. FC Lane is a BAE Systems Aircraft Business Units approved supplier (BAE/AG/20198/MAA) and holds a NATO NCAGE code (U0125). The Lodge Group umbrella covers FC Lane, Weald Electronics and Lane Motorsport.

## Products and solutions

- **Military circular connectors** - MIL-DTL-38999 Series I and III, MIL-DTL-26482, MIL-DTL-5015 and MIL-DTL-55302 connectors held in stock and available with full traceability.
- **RF and coaxial connectors and cable assemblies** - HUBER+SUHNER range for military communications and data link applications.
- **Fibre optic connectors and assemblies** - for defence and security performance-critical applications.
- **Custom connector assemblies** - in-house assembly and testing of military circular connectors to MIL-STD-790; specials design and manufacture through Weald Electronics.
- **Backshells and accessories** - circular connector backshells and accessories to AS85049.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (current 2026 certificate)
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2015 (FM588537); EN 9120:2018 / AS9120A (RS00100, distribution of electrical connectors); CECC/IECQ (distribution D2000, specialist contractor P4000, final assembly E003); QPL Category C (Eaton/Souriau approval, MIL-DTL-38999 Series I and III, micro38999, 8STA, JVS); Cyber Essentials (IASME-CE-040852); BAE Systems approved supplier (BAE/AG/20198/MAA SC4); NATO NCAGE U0125
- **Memberships:** ADS Group (ADS 7501, current 2026)

## Recent news

- **2026-04** - FC Lane celebrated its 60th anniversary in April 2026. [source](https://www.electronicsweekly.com/news/business/distribution/f-c-lane-celebrates-60th-anniversary-2026-04/)
- **2025-11** - Announced availability of CINCH Fibreco generic D38999 fibre-optic connectors for performance-critical applications. [source](https://www.fclane.com)

## Links

- Website: https://www.fclane.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fc-lane-electronics-ltd

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (fclane.com), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/f-c-lane-electronics-ltd-2/), Companies House (no. 00876597), Electronics Weekly, LinkedIn company page.

companies.name: Faculty Science Limited
companies.summary: London applied-AI company building and deploying AI systems for defence, national security and law enforcement; acquired by Accenture in March 2026.
companies.body:
# Faculty

> London applied-AI company building and deploying AI systems for defence, national security and law enforcement; acquired by Accenture in March 2026.

## What they do

Faculty is a UK applied artificial intelligence company that designs, builds and deploys AI systems for customers across defence, national security, government and commercial sectors. In defence and security it builds bespoke AI algorithms and customised off-the-shelf solutions spanning sensor enhancement, data exploitation and multi-domain command and control, with a stated emphasis on AI safety and ethical deployment. The company works with the UK Ministry of Defence, UK Strategic Command and national security and law enforcement partners. Faculty was acquired by Accenture, with the transaction completing 16 March 2026; it now operates as part of Accenture.

## Products and solutions

- **Cognitive Intelligent Sensing (CoInS)** - machine learning algorithms to autonomously process sensor data and orient sensing systems in real time; in joint development with Leonardo for unmanned platforms and ISR.
- **Electronic warfare AI** - AI capability acceleration for EW applications, developed in collaboration with Leonardo.
- **Defence AI systems** - bespoke and customised AI solutions spanning sensor enhancement, data exploitation and multi-domain command and control.
- **National security and law enforcement** - AI systems designed, built and deployed for operational use with UK security partners.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** G-Cloud (Digital Marketplace supplier ID 703150)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-03-16** - Acquisition by Accenture completed. [source](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2026/accenture-completes-acquisition-of-faculty)
- **2026-01-06** - Accenture announces agreement to acquire Faculty. [source](https://newsroom.accenture.com/news/2026/accenture-to-acquire-faculty)
- **2025-05-13** - Partnered with Leonardo to co-develop next-generation defence technologies, focused initially on Cognitive Intelligent Sensing and electronic warfare. [source](https://tech.eu/2025/05/13/leonardo-partners-with-london-based-faculty-ai-to-advance-defencetech/)

## Links

- Website: https://faculty.ai
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/facultyai

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (faculty.ai), Companies House (no. 08873131), ADS Group members directory, Accenture newsroom, Tech.eu, UK Defence Journal, Leonardo UK newsroom, Digital Marketplace (applytosupply.digitalmarketplace.service.gov.uk).

companies.name: Faltec Europe Limited
companies.summary: Sunderland precision plastics manufacturer supplying ground systems, aerospace defence and specialist equipment programmes; UK subsidiary of Tokyo-listed Faltec Co. Ltd.
companies.body:
# Faltec Europe

> Sunderland precision plastics manufacturer supplying ground systems, aerospace defence and specialist equipment programmes; UK subsidiary of Tokyo-listed Faltec Co. Ltd.

## What they do

Faltec Europe is a UK precision manufacturer based at the International Advanced Manufacturing Park (IAMP) in Sunderland, producing injection-moulded, co-extruded and assembled components for automotive, defence and aerospace customers. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Faltec Co. Ltd, listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and headquartered in Kawasaki, Japan. Its primary business is automotive Tier 1 supply, but it operates a dedicated defence sector offering covering ground systems, aerospace defence components and specialist mission equipment. Faltec Europe is JOSCAR-registered, holds ADS Group and Make UK Defence memberships, and has signed the Armed Forces Covenant. The company employs approximately 450 people at its Sunderland site.

## Products and solutions

- **Ground systems components** - internal and external vehicle system parts, structural and housing components, and lightweight shielding for military ground platforms.
- **Aerospace defence components** - structural and interior elements for defence aviation applications.
- **Specialist mission equipment parts** - bespoke injection-moulded components manufactured to customer specification for mission-specific requirements.
- **In-house manufacturing capabilities** - injection moulding (350 to 2,300 tonne presses), co-extrusion, assembly and automation, surface treatment, toolroom operations and precision testing.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (registered)
- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials; IATF 16949 (automotive quality, encompasses ISO 9001, BSI certified); ISO 14001 (LRQA certified); ISO 45001; Armed Forces Covenant signatory; FSQS registered
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Make UK Defence; NE RDSC (North East Reserve and Defence Support Community)

## Links

- Website: https://falteceurope.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/falteceurope

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (falteceurope.com), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/faltec-europe-ltd-2/), Companies House (no. 02385379), Faltec Co. Ltd investor information, LinkedIn company page.

companies.name: Far Field Exploits Limited
companies.summary: British RF engineering company designing low-profile antennas and resilient timing for the dismounted operator.
companies.body:
# Far Field Exploits

> British RF engineering company designing low-profile antennas and resilient timing for the dismounted operator.

## What they do

Far Field Exploits is a defence engineering company focused on enhancing tactical communications for the dismounted operator and the wider military and law enforcement community. It designs, develops and manufactures novel low-profile antennas for body-worn and vehicle applications, with RF engineering and microelectronics expertise that also supports research and development for government and industry partners. It is a veteran-owned business. Note: despite the name, this is an antenna and tactical-communications manufacturer, not a cyber or vulnerability-research firm; "far field" is an antenna-engineering term.

## Products and solutions

- **Sigma antenna range** - wideband, low-profile flexible antennas in tactical and covert body-worn variants, built on a patented Sigma antenna architecture.
- **Tachron** - precision wireless time and frequency transfer system providing GNSS-free synchronisation to within a few nanoseconds, resilient to jamming.
- **RF and antenna development services** - antenna design, testing and contract research and development for government and industry.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Programmes:** Completed a DASA Rapid Impact project (Tempus Wave); earlier jamming-resilience trials with Dstl.

## Recent news

- **2024-02-26** - Completed the DASA Rapid Impact project "Tempus Wave", integrating the Tachron timing system with surrogate electronic surveillance payloads for a field trial. [source](https://www.farfieldx.com/news-1/dasa-rampd-project-completed)

## Links

- Website: https://www.farfieldx.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/farfield-exploits-ltd

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (farfieldx.com), Companies House (no. 12097990), company news releases, Enforce Tac exhibitor listing.

companies.name: Farsound Aviation Limited
companies.summary: Specialist distributor and supply chain solutions provider for the aero engine MRO market.
companies.body:
# Farsound Aviation

> Specialist distributor and supply chain solutions provider for the aero engine MRO market.

## What they do

Farsound Aviation is a UK-headquartered procurement and supply chain company serving the aero engine maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) sector. It sources, manages and distributes high-volume consumable hardware components for engine overhaul programmes, including kitting, direct line feed and vending provision. The company operates a purpose-built 58,000 sq ft automated warehouse in Brentwood, Essex, with AI-driven robotic picking. It serves MRO organisations globally, some of which support military aviation programmes.

## Products and solutions

- **Consumable hardware supply** - procurement and distribution of high-volume aero engine hardware components for MRO operations.
- **Kitting and line-feed services** - pre-kitted component sets and direct-to-line delivery for engine overhaul facilities.
- **Inventory management** - vendor-managed inventory and vending solutions for MRO customers.
- **Global distribution network** - facilities in the UK, USA (San Antonio), Spain (Madrid), Canada, Singapore and Japan.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS/EN 9120B (aerospace distributor quality management); ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001; UK CAA AC00-56 (stores organisation approval); ASA-100 (Aviation Suppliers Association)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Midlands Aerospace Alliance

## Recent news

- **2026-06** - Exhibiting on the ADS Pavilion at Farnborough International Airshow 2026, showcasing global expansion and new Spain facility. [source](https://farsound.com/farsound-showcases-global-expansion-and-innovation-at-fia2026/)
- **2026-02** - Listed as a victim of the Akira ransomware group; breach reported February 2026. [source](https://www.hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-akira-hits-farsound-aviation/)
- **2025-04** - Exhibited at MRO Americas to develop US and Latin America customer relationships. [source](https://farsound.com/farsound-to-strengthen-customer-ties-across-the-usa-and-latin-america-by-exhibiting-at-mro-americas/)
- **2024-12** - Acquired by Onex Partners Opportunities Fund from AGIC Capital, alongside the Farsound management team. [source](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2024/08/29/2937699/0/en/Onex-Partners-to-Acquire-Farsound.html)
- **2024-09** - New 20,000 sq ft warehouse opened in San Antonio, Texas. [source](https://www.adsadvance.co.uk/farsound-expands-us-presence-with-new-texas-facility.html)

## Links

- Website: https://farsound.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/farsound-aviation-limited

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (farsound.com), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/farsound-1/), Companies House (no. 01989923), GlobeNewswire acquisition announcement, ADS Advance, MRO Business Today, Hookphish ransomware tracker.

companies.name: FarX
companies.summary: Early-stage biometric AI company developing fused voice, face, and speech recognition for continuous multi-factor identity authentication.
companies.body:
# FarX

> Early-stage biometric AI company developing fused voice, face, and speech recognition for continuous multi-factor identity authentication.

## What they do

FarX builds biometric authentication technology that fuses voice, speech, and face recognition into a continuous multi-factor identity verification system. The company is based in Great Malvern, Worcestershire, within the UK's established cyber and signals intelligence cluster. Its technology targets government and law enforcement applications, with explicit capability for deepfake and synthetic voice detection.

## Products and solutions

- **Fused biometric authentication** - continuous identity verification combining voice recognition, speech analysis, and facial recognition into a single multi-factor system.
- **Synthetic media detection** - capability to identify deepfake video and synthetic voice audio, relevant to counter-disinformation and identity assurance applications.

## Links

- Website: https://www.farx.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/farx-group-limited

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (farx.co.uk); Companies House (FarX Group Limited 14340528; related entities FarX Operations (UK) Limited 13742433 and FarX Technologies Limited 13742474).

companies.name: Fat Potato Ltd
companies.summary: Defence-sector software consultancy specialising in cloud-native development, AWS migration and secure-by-design engineering.
companies.body:
# Fat Potato

> Defence-sector software consultancy specialising in cloud-native development, AWS migration and secure-by-design engineering.

## What they do

Fat Potato is a Cheltenham-based software consultancy that has operated primarily in the defence sector since its founding in 2016. The company delivers bespoke cloud-native software development, legacy system migration to AWS, and secure-by-design engineering for government and defence clients. It is an AWS Partner and is approved on the MOD's Neutral Vendor Framework for Innovation (Constellia NVFi). The company also develops an in-house HR platform product, Chinchill.HR.

## Products and solutions

- **Bespoke software development** - cloud-native, microservices-based software for defence and government customers.
- **AWS cloud transformation** - migration of legacy systems to Amazon Web Services, including architecture design and delivery.
- **Chinchill.HR** - in-house HR platform product.
- **Cyber resiliency and secure design** - Cyber Essentials Plus certified; applies secure-by-design principles across its delivery work.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** MOD Neutral Vendor Framework for Innovation (Constellia NVFi)
- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials Plus; AWS Partner (AWS Partner Network)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; JOSCAR registered

## Recent news

- **2025** - Finalist in the SoGlos Gloucestershire Business Awards 2025, shortlisted for Best Place to Work. [source](https://www.soglos.com/news/business/gloucestershire-business-awards-2025-winners-revealed/24441/)

companies.name: FAUN Trackway Limited
companies.summary: Designer and manufacturer of portable aluminium Trackway ground stabilisation systems for military and specialist civil applications.
companies.body:
# FAUN Trackway

> Designer and manufacturer of portable aluminium Trackway ground stabilisation systems for military and specialist civil applications.

## What they do

FAUN Trackway is a Welsh manufacturer of portable, recoverable Trackway panel systems used by military engineers worldwide to provide rapid access across soft ground, mud, marsh, snow and sand. Originating in wartime aluminium fabrication on the Isle of Anglesey from 1940, the company has supplied the UK MOD since the 1960s and exports to numerous NATO and partner-nation armed forces. It operates three divisions: Trackway Defence, Trackway Access (civil) and Trackway Engineering. The company is part of the German KIRCHHOFF Group (FAUN KIRCHHOFF GRUPPE). A US subsidiary, FAUN Trackway USA Inc., is based in Washington DC.

## Products and solutions

- **Military Trackway panels** - aluminium alloy portable roadway panels rated to MLC 30, MLC 60, MLC 70 and MLC 150; recoverable and redeployable.
- **Heavy Ground Mobility System (HGMS)** - vehicle-mounted rapid deployment and recovery system for high-load Trackway.
- **Medium Ground Mobility System (MGMS)** - vehicle-mounted system for medium-load Trackway deployment.
- **Airfield Damage Repair mats** - aluminium matting for rapid airfield surface repair.
- **Helicopter landing systems** - Containerised Helipad ("Helipad in a Box") and UAV and aircraft landing pads.
- **Over-the-shore access matting** - marine ingress and egress systems.
- **Engineering services** - bespoke manufacturing for defence and nuclear sectors.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001:2015; ISO 14001:2015; ISO 45001:2018; BS EN 1090 Execution Class 4 (structural aluminium fabrication); Fit For Nuclear (F4N, granted 2018); Investors in People
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-04** - Signed the Armed Forces Covenant with RAF Valley (Station Commander Group Captain Currie OBE). [source](https://fauntrackway.co.uk/latest-news/2025/faun-trackway-limited-signs-the-armed-covenant)
- **2023** - Signed a multi-million-pound contract with the Dutch Ministry of Defence to supply Heavy Ground Mobility Systems to the Royal Netherlands Army; final delivery running into 2025. [source](https://fauntrackway.co.uk/latest-news/2023/faun-trackway-limited-signs-multi-million-pound-contract-for-dutch-ministry-of-defence)

## Links

- Website: https://fauntrackway.co.uk
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/faun-trackway

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (fauntrackway.co.uk), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/faun-trackway-limited-2/), Companies House (no. 09283749), GOV.UK Armed Forces Covenant register, FAUN Trackway news pages.

# Fearnaught

> Tri-service defence consultancy of retired senior military officers advising defence companies on UK MOD engagement and procurement.

## What they do

Fearnaught is a Hampshire-based consultancy comprising retired senior British military officers drawn from across the three services. It supports defence companies seeking to develop and market their capabilities to the UK Ministry of Defence, providing insight into MOD procurement processes, acquisition pathways and customer relationships. The company is registered with ADS Group under its Defence Consultancy Services capability category and is based in Odiham, Hampshire, close to RAF Odiham.

companies.name: FEL Avionics Limited
companies.summary: Specialist stockist, repairer and support provider for legacy defence radar systems, holding over 16,300 registered NATO/NSN part numbers.
companies.body:
# FEL Avionics

> Specialist stockist, repairer and support provider for legacy defence radar systems, holding over 16,300 registered NATO/NSN part numbers.

## What they do

FEL Avionics is a subsidiary of UKC Group, operating as a stockist and distributor of discontinued piece parts and line-replaceable units for legacy defence radar systems. The company provides repair and overhaul services, onsite fault diagnosis, technician training, and a travelling wave tube test facility. Where components are globally unavailable, the team reverse-engineers and manufactures small batches of critical parts.

## Products and solutions

- **Legacy radar spares** - stock supply of discontinued components and LRUs for BAE/Plessey Radar Systems (S511, Martello, Watchman, AR-51, AR325, AR327), naval radar (994, 996), ground radar (AR3D), and US Army radars AN/TPS-70 and AN/TPS-43; also GEC Marconi, Racal Decca, and Marine Avionics lines.
- **Repair and overhaul** - fault-finding, repair, and full overhaul by technicians trained at Plessey, BAE, Marconi, and AMS.
- **TWT test facility** - Travelling Wave Tube testing capability for radar sub-assembly verification.
- **Reverse engineering and batch manufacture** - small-batch manufacture of globally unavailable critical components.
- **Lease and lend programme** - partnership plan for specialised sub-assemblies to support continuity of operations.
- **Technician training** - courses at all levels for covered radar systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, Cyber Essentials, Cyber Essentials Plus
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- Website: https://www.fel-avionics.com/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fel-avionics-ltd

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. FEL Avionics website (fel-avionics.com), ADS Group member profile (adsgroup.org.uk/members/fel-avionics-ltd-1), Companies House (11820517), UKC Group website (ukc.group/fel-avionics, ukc.group/accreditations), LinkedIn company page.

companies.name: Fenix Insight Ltd
companies.summary: Open-source intelligence company specialising in munitions, weapons systems, and global conflict event data, serving defence, government, and humanitarian customers.
companies.body:
# Fenix Insight

> Open-source intelligence company specialising in munitions, weapons systems, and global conflict event data, serving defence, government, and humanitarian customers.

## What they do

Fenix Insight combines explosive ordnance expertise with AI and data analytics to produce structured intelligence on munitions, weapons platforms, and conflict events. Founded in 2011, the company serves UK and international civil and military government departments, humanitarian organisations, and commercial risk management customers. In addition to its data products, the company delivers specialist consultancy, capability development, incident investigation, and evidence-based policy design.

## Products and solutions

- **METIS** (Munitions, Events and Technical Information System) - the company's primary OSINT platform, launched in 2015 and rebranded to METIS in 2025. Covers over 470,000 geospatial conflict events, 6,700 technical munition, platform, and CBRN records, and 45,000 images. Delivered via secure web interface or API.
- **IRIS** (Image Recognition Identification System) - machine vision software that detects landmines and explosive ordnance from UAV video feeds in real time. Field-trialled with Ukrainian Army Engineers and the Ukrainian Ministry of Emergencies in January 2024.
- **Intelligence briefings** - monthly SITREPs, munitions newsletters, and CBRN bulletins.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, Cyber Essentials Plus
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-12-11** - Monthly METIS munitions newsletter published. [source](https://www.fenixinsight.com/blog/newsletters/)
- **2025-11-12** - Monthly METIS munitions newsletter published. [source](https://www.fenixinsight.com/blog/newsletters/)

- **2025** - METIS platform rebranded from Fenix Insight Online, reflecting AI integration since 2015. [source](https://www.fenixinsight.com/about-us)

companies.name: Fenner Precision Polymers
companies.summary: Manufacturer of engineered polymer products including aerospace-grade ducting and coated fabrics, operating as a Michelin Group subsidiary with its UK manufacturing base in Lincoln.
companies.body:
# Fenner Precision Polymers

> Manufacturer of engineered polymer products including aerospace-grade ducting and coated fabrics, operating as a Michelin Group subsidiary with its UK manufacturing base in Lincoln.

## What they do

Fenner Precision Polymers manufactures engineered polymer components across three global product families: industrial motion (timing and power transmission belts), high-tech coated fabrics, and air and fluid handling products. The Lincoln, UK site holds AS9100 Rev D accreditation for aerospace and defence manufacturing, covering ducting under the James Dawson brand and specialist coated fabrics and textiles under the Critical Thermal Composites brand. Fenner joined ADS Group in August 2025 to target growth in the aerospace, defence, and space markets. The company is wholly owned by Michelin Group, which acquired Fenner plc in May 2018.

## Products and solutions

- **Aerospace ducting** (James Dawson brand) - air and fluid handling products for aerospace and defence applications, manufactured at Lincoln under AS9100 Rev D.
- **Critical thermal composites** (Critical Thermal Composites brand) - specialist coated fabrics and textiles for thermal management in aerospace and defence environments, manufactured at Lincoln under AS9100 Rev D.
- **Industrial motion products** (Fenner Precision brand) - timing belts and power transmission components for wider industrial markets.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, AS9100 Rev D (Lincoln site, scoped to aerospace and defence manufacturing of ducting and coated fabrics; achieved December 2025)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-01** - AS9100 Rev D accreditation achieved at the Lincoln site for aerospace and defence manufacturing. [ADS Advance](https://www.adsadvance.co.uk/fenner-precision-polymers-achieves-as9100-accreditation.html) | [East Midlands Business Link](https://www.eastmidlandsbusinesslink.co.uk/mag/featured/fenner-site-in-lincoln-secures-as9100-for-aerospace-and-defence-production/)
- **2025-08** - Joined ADS Group, targeting aerospace, defence, and space sector growth. [European Rubber Journal](https://www.european-rubber-journal.com/article/2097902/fenner-targets-aerospace-growth-join-s-uk-trade-group-ads)

## Links

companies.name: Fenrir Solutions Ltd
companies.summary: Electronic warfare modelling, simulation and testing consultancy serving the defence sector.
companies.body:
# Fenrir Solutions

> Electronic warfare modelling, simulation and testing consultancy serving the defence sector.

## What they do

Fenrir Solutions is a specialist consultancy in electronic warfare modelling, simulation, and testing. The company draws on over 20 years of military experience and over a decade of electronic warfare expertise, delivering modelling and simulation services to defence customers. It is listed in the ADS Group member directory under simulation and modelling services, based in London.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

companies.name: Ferchau Limited

companies.body:
# Ferchau

> German-headquartered engineering services group delivering specialist aerospace and defence engineering from its UK base at Bournemouth Airport; Airbus Preferred Supplier for engineering and customer services.

## What they do

Ferchau Limited is the UK entity of FERCHAU GmbH, a privately held German engineering services group founded in 1966. The UK operation, incorporated in January 2011, is based at Aviation Park West, Bournemouth Airport, with a second site in Bristol. It delivers specialist engineering services to the aerospace and defence supply chain, including structural analysis, systems design (fuel, hydraulics, and electrical systems), vibration and operational stability analyses, aerospace certification support, and embedded engineering and testing. The group's Aviation Group subsidiary RST Rostock manufactures harness test systems and cabin simulators for military platforms including Eurofighter. FERCHAU is not listed as an ADS Group member.

## Products and solutions

- **Structural and systems engineering** - stress calculations, design verification, systems design for aerospace and defence applications, delivered from Bournemouth and Bristol.
- **Certification support** - specialist assistance through the Airbus and wider aerospace regulatory certification process.
- **Embedded engineering and test** - embedded development and testing capability for aerospace and defence programmes.
- **Harness test systems and cabin simulators** (RST Rostock, group subsidiary) - manufactured test equipment and simulators for civil, military, and space programmes including Eurofighter.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, AS9100 (aviation division), ISO 27001 (aviation division), ISO 14001, ISO 45001

## Recent news

- **2026-05-07** - FERCHAU presented at AFCEA TechNet International 2026 on supporting defence companies with complex programme implementation. [source](https://www.ferchau.com/de/en/press/07-05-2026-afcea-2026-how-ferchau-supports-defense-companies-in-implementing-complex-programs-2362)
- **2026-03-11** - Group announced FY2025 revenue of €945 million, with focus shifting to aviation, energy, and future industries. [source](https://www.ferchau.com/de/de/presse/11-03-2026-ferchau-group-trotzt-der-schwierigen-marktlage-und-richtet-fokus-auf-zukunftsindustrien-2323)

companies.name: Fergusson's Advanced Composite Technology Limited
companies.summary: London deep-tech R&D company developing advanced composite materials and adhesive systems for military aviation, aerospace and space applications.
companies.body:
# FAC Technology

> London deep-tech R&D company developing advanced composite materials and adhesive systems for military aviation, aerospace and space applications.

## What they do

FAC Technology (trading name of Fergusson's Advanced Composite Technology Limited) is a specialist research and development company working at the molecular level of advanced composite materials. Founded by Dr Alexander Fergusson, an aerospace composites researcher from Imperial College London, where he holds Honorary Lecturer and Academic Visitor status, the company develops high-performance composite materials, structural adhesives and potting compounds with documented application in civil and military aviation, satellite manufacturing and space systems. FAC Technology holds Civil Aviation Authority Alternative Design Organisation approval (UK.AP1002) and has achieved ETSO C90d certification for its composite air-cargo pallet. The company participated in the Advanced Propulsion Centre Technology Developer Accelerator Programme (TDAP), raising technology readiness from TRL 3 to TRL 8.

## Products and solutions

- **Deep Air Carbon (DAC) composite materials** - structural composite material system in use in civil and military aviation applications; forms the basis of the ETSO C90d-approved composite air-cargo pallet (PMC type).
- **Fire-resistant composites** - flame penetration resistant materials with a burn rate of 0 mm/min, relevant to aircraft and military vehicle applications.
- **Advanced structural adhesives** - developed for satellite manufacturing, confirmed for use in critical space applications with a leading satellite manufacturer.
- **Motor resins** - high-thermal-performance materials for electric motor applications including UAV and propulsion systems.
- **Potting compounds** - specialist encapsulation compounds for aerospace and space applications.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** CAA Alternative Design Organisation (UK.AP1002); ETSO C90d product approval (composite air-cargo pallet); Composites UK member
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fac-technology

Company website (factechnology.co.uk), ADS Group members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/fac-technology-ltd-2/), Companies House (no. 07591421), Composites UK hub, Advanced Propulsion Centre funded projects, Strategic Allies tech spotlight, LinkedIn company page.

companies.body:
# Fieldfisher

> International law firm advising the defence, aerospace and space sectors, with active programmes across F-35, satellite, and defence technology.

## What they do

Fieldfisher LLP is a multinational law firm providing legal services across commercial, regulatory, transactional, and technology matters. Its headline sectors include technology, financial services, energy and natural resources, and life sciences. Within those it runs specialist practices in satellite and space, international trade and sanctions, intellectual property, defence supply chain, and real estate. The firm is an ADS Group member and has a documented track record advising defence primes, MOD joint ventures, and national-security-focused space companies.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence programme legal advisory** - commercial, supply chain, IP, real estate, health and safety and regulatory advice on major defence programmes, including the F-35 global maintenance, repair, overhaul and upgrade programme for the BAE Systems / Northrop Grumman / MOD joint venture Sealand Support Services Limited.
- **Space and satellite law** - advising on satellite launches, regulatory matters, and national security space projects; acted for SatVu on the HotSat-2 launch (SpaceX Transporter-16, March 2026), a satellite carrying mid-wave infrared sensors for national security applications.
- **International trade and sanctions** - regulatory advice relevant to defence exports, dual-use goods, and Russia sanctions.
- **Defence technology procurement analysis** - published analysis on MOD's Defence AI Centre (DAIC) AI Model Arena and technology procurement processes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; BDSV (Bundesverband der Deutschen Sicherheits- und Verteidigungsindustrie, German security and defence industry association)

## Recent news

- **2026-04** - Advised SatVu on the HotSat-2 satellite launch from Vandenberg Space Force Base, carrying national security-oriented infrared sensors. [source](https://www.law.com/international-edition/2026/04/10/fieldfisher-leaps-into-space-advising-on-landmark-uk-satellite-launch/)

companies.name: Fife College
companies.summary: Scottish further and higher education college delivering defence workforce development and advanced manufacturing training, with a campus embedded in Babcock's Rosyth Dockyard.
companies.body:
# Fife College

> Scottish further and higher education college delivering defence workforce development and advanced manufacturing training, with a campus embedded in Babcock's Rosyth Dockyard.

## What they do

Fife College is a Scottish further and higher education institution offering over 400 courses across engineering, construction, digital technologies, health and social care, business, and creative industries. It serves more than 20,000 students annually and runs over 420 employer partnerships. The college's Rosyth Engineering Campus sits inside Babcock International's Rosyth Dockyard on the Firth of Forth, with approximately 10,000 sq m of purpose-built training facilities focused on engineering, renewables, digital, and advanced manufacturing. The college is one of six partners in the Arrol-Gibb Innovation Campus (AGIC) at Rosyth, alongside the Universities of Edinburgh and Strathclyde, Scottish Enterprise, Skills Development Scotland, and Fife Council.

## Products and solutions

- **Defence workforce development** - bespoke programmes supplying upskilled STEM workers for the Type 31 frigate programme and other national and international defence programmes at Rosyth; includes Production Support Operative (PSO) and Trainee Welder pathways delivered under the Babcock MoU.
- **Advanced manufacturing and engineering training** - apprenticeships and programmes covering composites, robotics, nuclear manufacturing, and digital shipbuilding at the Rosyth campus.
- **Arrol-Gibb Innovation Campus** - collaborative research and training campus at Rosyth supporting the Type 31 build across advanced manufacturing, digital shipbuilding, and related disciplines.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-03** - Fife College and Forth Valley College signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Babcock International Group during Scottish Apprenticeship Week, formalising a talent pipeline for the Type 31 frigate programme at Rosyth. UK Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry Luke Pollard MP attended. [source](https://www.babcockinternational.com/news/babcock-launches-joint-skills-mou-with-fife-and-forth-valley-colleges-to-boost-scotlands-defence-workforce/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fife-college
