companies.body:
# Filtronic

> AIM-listed designer and manufacturer of RF, microwave, and millimetre-wave components and subsystems for defence, space, and telecoms.

## What they do

Filtronic is a UK public company that designs and manufactures high-performance radio frequency, microwave, and millimetre-wave components and subsystems. It serves the defence and aerospace, space, telecoms infrastructure, and critical communications markets. Core capabilities include RF design, solid-state power amplifiers (SSPAs), transmit/receive modules, hybrid manufacturing, GaAs and GaN semiconductor assembly and test, plastic encapsulation of compound semiconductor devices, and electromechanical manufacturing. Principal sites are at NETPark Sedgefield in County Durham; additional UK design offices are in Leeds, Manchester, and Cambridge. The company also operates a manufacturing site in Salisbury, Maryland, USA. It is listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM market under the ticker FTC.

## Products and solutions

- **Transmit/receive modules** - radar T/R modules for defence customers; the company passed 60,000 units in production as of 2025.
- **Solid-state power amplifiers** - GaN-based SSPAs for space (including E-Band SSPAs for SpaceX Starlink) and defence applications.
- **Electronic sensor system components** - bespoke RF assemblies for defence prime contractors; a £13.4 million contract for an electronic sensor system was announced in July 2025.
- **Compound semiconductor packaging** - plastic encapsulation of GaAs and GaN devices for high-density System-in-Package designs; DASA DTEP award for low-cost, low-SWAP packaging for future radar applications.
- **Satellite communications RF assemblies** - RF assemblies for low-earth-orbit satellite constellations including Airbus LEO programmes.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001 (UK and USA operations); ISO 14001 (Sedgefield sites); IASME Governance Gold
- **Programmes:** DASA Defence Technology Exploitation Programme (DTEP) award recipient
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-02** - Interim results for the six months to November 2025: revenue £25.3 million; record order book; active programmes with Leonardo, QinetiQ, and BAE Maritime Systems confirmed. [source](https://filtronic.com/investors/)
- **2025-08** - £47.3 million contract from SpaceX for next-generation GaN E-Band SSPAs for the Starlink constellation; described at the time as the largest contract in the company's history. [source](https://filtronic.com/news-events/news/filtronic-secures-record-breaking-spacex-order/)
- **2025-07** - £13.4 million contract to supply components for an electronic sensor system for a European defence prime. [source](https://bdaily.co.uk/articles/2025/07/14/filtronic-secures-134-million-defence-deal)

## Links

- Website: https://www.filtronic.com
- LinkedIn: https://uk.linkedin.com/company/filtronic

companies.name: First Light Fusion Ltd
companies.summary: Oxford University spinout commercialising hypervelocity amplifier technology for defence, space, and fusion research customers.
companies.body:
# First Light Fusion

> Oxford University spinout commercialising hypervelocity amplifier technology for defence, space, and fusion research customers.

## What they do

First Light Fusion developed inertial confinement fusion technology using projectile-based and pulsed-power approaches. Following a strategic pivot in 2025, the company now operates two commercial lines: licensing its amplifier technology to other fusion ventures, and selling and deploying its experimental infrastructure to defence, space, and research customers. Their VIPER velocity amplifier and supporting facilities are explicitly positioned for warhead assurance, armour and materials qualification, and micro-meteoroid and orbital debris testing.

## Products and solutions

- **VIPER velocity amplifier** - a device that boosts projectile velocity beyond 10 km/s; first commercial sale delivered to Texas A&M HVIL in June 2026. Stated applications include hypervelocity impact testing, orbital debris simulation, and warhead assurance.
- **Two-stage gas gun** - the UK's largest, used for hypervelocity launch and impact testing.
- **Pulsed power facility** - Europe's largest pulsed power facility, used for high-energy physics, propulsion research, and extreme-environment testing relevant to defence materials science.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR (registered January 2026)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group; UKspace (start-up member)

## Recent news

- **2026-06-01** - First experimental campaign with VIPER completed at Texas A&M HVIL; hypervelocity impacts above 12 km/s achieved. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-04-01** - £25m funding round first close led by Starmaker One and East X Ventures; strategic investment from UKAEA. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-02-01** - AI collaboration with Locai Labs announced alongside tritium breeding performance validation. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)
- **2026-01-01** - JOSCAR registration confirmed. [ADS Group](https://www.adsgroup.org.uk)
- **2025-08-01** - World record pressure set on two-stage gas gun. [First Light Fusion](https://firstlightfusion.com)

## Links

- Website: https://firstlightfusion.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/first-light-fusion

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. Company website (firstlightfusion.com), ADS members directory (adsgroup.org.uk/members/first-light-fusion-ltd-2), UKspace member directory, Companies House (07555858), published press releases and news coverage 2025-2026.

First Response (Europe) provides digital forensics, cyber incident response, and cybercrime investigation services. companies.body:
# Fischer Connectors

> Swiss-headquartered manufacturer of high-performance electrical and fibre-optic connectors for military, aerospace, and industrial applications; UK entity based in Waterlooville, Hampshire.

## What they do

Fischer Connectors designs, manufactures, and distributes electrical connectors, fibre-optic connectors, cable assemblies, and connectivity systems for demanding environments. Defence is a primary vertical: products are tested to MIL-SPEC and IEC standards and are used across dismounted soldier, ground vehicle, aerospace, and marine applications. The company operates under the parent group brand Conextivity Group, with the Swiss parent Fischer Connectors SA as the global headquarters. The UK entity (Fischer Connectors Ltd, incorporated 1988) handles sales and distribution for the British market.

## Products and solutions

- **KEYSTONE tactical hub** - a connectivity platform built for the Internet of Battlefield Things (IoBT), with NATO STANAG compatibility and Nett Warrior integration.
- **MiniMax 06** - an ultra-miniature connector targeting handheld defence devices and UAVs.
- **SS Series and SSC Series** - short-plug miniature connectors for high pin-density, space-saving military and instrumentation use (launched January 2026).
- **Fibre-optic connectors and cable assemblies** - for harsh-environment military communications and sensor systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001; ISO 14001; ISO 13485; Cyber Essentials; Cyber Essentials Plus

companies.summary: AI-powered open-source intelligence platform for national security, defence, and law enforcement missions.
companies.body:
# Fivecast UK

> AI-powered open-source intelligence platform for national security, defence, and law enforcement missions.

## What they do

Fivecast is an Australian-founded digital intelligence company that develops AI-enabled OSINT software for government and enterprise customers across national security, defence, law enforcement, border security, and financial crime prevention. The company was founded in 2017 from collaboration between Australian government agencies and research institutions, and has expanded into the United Kingdom and United States. Fivecast UK Ltd is the UK legal entity (Companies House 13861067), serving UK and European customers from a London-based team as part of the company's Five Eyes market expansion.

## Products and solutions

- **Fivecast ONYX** - OSINT collection and analysis platform for broad discovery, targeted data collection, and near real-time insights across millions of online sources.
- **Fivecast LUNEX** - analyst-curated dataset product that surfaces hidden threat networks and delivers rapid intelligence, integrated into the ONYX platform.
- **Fivecast MATRIX** - large-scale risk assessment solution for security vetting and screening, developed in conjunction with the US Government.
- **Fivecast Discovery** - automated digital footprinting, identity resolution, and information verification capability integrated within ONYX.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Foreign-headquartered (Adelaide, Australia) with a UK legal entity (Fivecast UK Ltd, Companies House 13861067) and a London-based team. Also operates in Washington DC, USA.

## Recent news

- **2026-03-00** - Fivecast exhibited at Security and Policing 2026, the UK Home Office-hosted closed event for national security and law enforcement. [source](https://www.fivecast.com/news/security-and-policing-2026/)
- **2024-10-14** - Fivecast launched Discovery, a new OSINT capability for digital footprinting and identity resolution within the ONYX platform. [source](https://uktechnews.co.uk/2024/10/14/fivecast-launches-discovery-to-tackle-global-threats-with-osint-innovation/)

## Links

# Flann Microwave

> Designer and manufacturer of RF, microwave and millimetre-wave components and precision test instrumentation.

## What they do

Flann Microwave is a UK manufacturer of precision passive RF, microwave and millimetre-wave components, sub-systems and calibration instrumentation, operating from around 300 MHz to 1.1 THz. Trading since 1956 and based in Bodmin, Cornwall, it serves aerospace and defence, space and satellite communications, metrology and test, telecommunications, and research markets, with the majority of production exported. It employs around 65 staff.

companies.summary: Machine learning and digital twin software for GNSS-denied drone navigation, testing, and evaluation.
companies.body:
# Flare Bright

> Machine learning and digital twin software for GNSS-denied drone navigation, testing, and evaluation.

## What they do

Flare Bright develops machine learning software and digital twin technology for unmanned aerial and undersea vehicles. Founded in November 2015 and based at Westcott Venture Park in Buckinghamshire, the company builds software that enables drones to fly accurately in GNSS-denied environments, perform in-flight wind sensing, and support regulatory safety case assurance. Flare Bright has shifted from developing its own drone hardware to providing software that integrates with existing and future platforms built by defence primes and other manufacturers. It holds contracts with the UK MOD, Dstl, and the US Department of Defense.

## Products and solutions

- **Digital twin platform** - software environment that models unmanned aircraft and applies machine learning to solve navigation, control, sensing, and optimisation problems, enabling GNSS-denied flight.
- **Extera** - containment assurance software for critical drone operations, designed to accelerate testing and evaluation programmes for novel drone systems; introduced at DSEI 2025.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Recipient of multiple DASA Rapid Innovation contracts for GNSS-denied flight capability development. Fourth-sprint project with the US Department of Defense at time of most recent reporting.

## Recent news

- **2025-09-00** - Flare Bright introduced Extera, containment software for defence testing and evaluation, at DSEI 2025 in London. [source](https://www.adsadvance.co.uk/flare-bright-introduces-extera-at-dsei.html)
- **2023-12-00** - Flare Bright's digital twin software entered trials with the US Department of Defense in Spain using a CATUAV drone in GNSS-denied conditions. [source](https://www.govconexec.com/2023/12/flare-brights-digital-twin-software-currently-undergoing-tests-in-us-spain/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/flarebright

companies.name: Fleetwind Ltd
companies.summary: ISO 9001-accredited naval systems engineering consultancy specialising in platform integration, safety assurance, and in-service support for complex warship programmes.
companies.body:
# Fleetwind

> ISO 9001-accredited naval systems engineering consultancy specialising in platform integration, safety assurance, and in-service support for complex warship programmes.

## What they do

Fleetwind Ltd is a maritime systems engineering company formed in 2019 and registered in Polruan, Cornwall. The company works directly with the Royal Navy, UK MOD, and defence primes across the supply chain, with its business predominantly in the defence sector. Fleetwind specialises in the integration and sustainment of complex naval platforms, covering the transition of ships and systems from the build environment into service. The team is drawn from ex-Naval personnel, business leaders, and specialist engineers with experience on high-profile programmes.

## Products and solutions

- **Platform integration and assurance** - systems engineering support for the transition of naval platforms and systems into the in-service environment, including Type 26, Type 31, and QE-Class programmes.
- **Safety case and certification** - delivery of Safety Case Reports, Naval Authority Certification submissions, and supporting assurance across Ordnance, Munitions and Explosives (OME), aviation safety, and integrated platform assurance for surface and sub-surface environments.
- **Ship-Air Integration** - development of Interface Control Documents, Ship Aviation Safety Case Reports, and Ship Air Release recommendations, supporting Naval Authority certification submissions.
- **In-service support** - integrated logistic support (ILS) disciplines, in-service support solution development, and business management advice.

companies.name: FLIR Systems Limited
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR, designing and manufacturing thermal imaging, surveillance sensors, and uncrewed systems for defence, security, and commercial customers.
companies.body:
# FLIR Systems

> UK subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR, designing and manufacturing thermal imaging, surveillance sensors, and uncrewed systems for defence, security, and commercial customers.

## What they do

FLIR Systems Limited is the UK-registered subsidiary of Teledyne FLIR LLC, the defence and sensing division of Teledyne Technologies Incorporated (NYSE: TDY). The UK entity was incorporated in 1977 and operates from sites in Kent and Chelmsford. FLIR supplies thermography, night vision, and surveillance equipment to military, law enforcement, and commercial customers, with a defence portfolio spanning thermal imaging for vehicles, aircraft, and dismounted soldiers, autonomous uncrewed aerial systems, and chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosives (CBRNE) detection. Teledyne completed its acquisition of the parent company, FLIR Systems Inc., in May 2021 for approximately 8.2 billion USD.

## Products and solutions

- **ThermoVision SA90-HD** - upgraded situational awareness vehicle vision system launched at Eurosatory 2026, providing HD thermal imaging for military vehicles.
- **ThermoVision DV55-HD** - HD driver vision system for military vehicles, announced alongside the SA90-HD in June 2026.
- **MilSight LIRC III** - compact vehicle-mounted weapon sight, announced at Eurosatory 2026.
- **SkyRaider and SkyRanger UAS** - tactical uncrewed aerial systems for reconnaissance and persistent surveillance.
- **SkyCarrier** - autonomous UAS launch and recovery platform for deployment from vehicles, vessels, or fixed sites, introduced at DSEI 2025.
- **Black Recon** - autonomous micro-drone system for vehicle-based reconnaissance, weighing under 450 grams; launched June 2026.
- **Handheld and airborne sensors** - thermal imaging and visible-spectrum sensor systems for soldier, maritime, and airborne applications.
- **CBRNE detectors** - chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosives detection systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Foreign-headquartered (Wilsonville, Oregon, USA). UK entity FLIR Systems Limited (Companies House 01320288) incorporated 6 July 1977. Registered office: 2 Kings Hill Avenue, Kings Hill, West Malling, Kent. UK correspondence: 106 Waterhouse Lane, Chelmsford. Parent company Teledyne Technologies listed on NYSE.

## Recent news

- **2026-06-15** - Teledyne FLIR Defense launched Black Recon autonomous micro-drone system and unveiled three upgraded vehicle vision systems at Eurosatory 2026. [source](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260615122823/en/Teledyne-FLIR-Defense-Unveils-Three-Vehicle-Vision-Systems-for-Enhanced-Battlefield-Awareness)
- **2025-09-00** - Teledyne FLIR unveiled the SkyCarrier autonomous UAS launch and recovery platform at DSEI in London. [source](https://defense.flir.com/about/news/teledyne-flir-defense-unveils-skycarrier-autonomous-uas-launch-and-recovery-platform-at-dsei-in-london/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.flir.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/teledyne-flir

## Sources

ADS Group member directory (adsgroup.org.uk), Companies House (FLIR SYSTEMS LIMITED, company number 01320288, incorporated 6 July 1977, registered Kent), Teledyne Technologies acquisition announcement (May 2021), Business Wire (Eurosatory 2026 announcements June 2026), Teledyne FLIR Defense press releases (SkyCarrier DSEI 2025, Black Recon June 2026), CAAT (UK entity data), Airframer (UK profile).

companies.name: Flitetec Limited
companies.summary: Aerospace manufacturer and distributor of aircraft interior components and assemblies for civil and military fixed-wing and rotary platforms.
companies.body:
# Flitetec

> Aerospace manufacturer and distributor of aircraft interior components and assemblies for civil and military fixed-wing and rotary platforms.

## What they do

Flitetec Limited was established in 1985 and is headquartered in Amersham, Buckinghamshire, with additional operations in Hampshire and south-west France. The company designs, manufactures, repairs, overhauls, sources, and integrates aircraft interior products for both civil and military aircraft, including fixed-wing and helicopter platforms. Flitetec also operates an aerospace distribution service, and holds CAA Part 21G manufacture approval, Part 145 maintenance approval, and EASA Part 145 maintenance approval, with quality management certified to AS9100 D by Lloyds Register.

## Products and solutions

- **Aircraft interior manufacture and repair** - design, manufacture, and overhaul of aircraft interior components including seating products, galley assemblies, lavatory modules, fairings, meal trays, and specialist interior fittings for civil and military platforms.
- **Aerospace distribution** - sourcing and distribution of aviation components and products for defence and commercial aircraft applications across approximately 100 countries.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS9100 D (Lloyds Register), CAA Part 21G, CAA Part 145, EASA Part 145, Cyber Essentials
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

- **2022-09-00** - Flitetec completed the acquisition of most of Aeropair Limited's assets, strengthening its source, make, and integrate capability. [source](https://runwaygirlnetwork.com/2022/09/flitetec-completes-business-acquisition/)

companies.name: Flowcopter Ltd
companies.summary: Scottish developer of hydraulic-drive heavy-lift cargo drones for long-range missions.
companies.body:
# Flowcopter

> Scottish developer of hydraulic-drive heavy-lift cargo drones for long-range missions.

## What they do

Flowcopter designs and manufactures heavy-lift uncrewed air systems built around digital hydraulic drive technology, a spin-out from Artemis Intelligent Power. Its platforms are aimed at markets traditionally served by helicopters, including offshore logistics, renewables, agriculture, emergency response and humanitarian aid. The company pairs combustion engines with digital displacement pumps feeding hydraulic motors that regulate propeller speed.

## Products and solutions

- **FC100** - heavy-lift cargo drone designed to carry payloads up to 100 kg over distances around 200 km with multi-hour endurance, positioned as a lower-fuel alternative to helicopters.

## Recent news

- **2024-05** - Completed flight testing of its heavy-lift cargo drone at HCA Airport in Denmark, its European test base. [source](https://dronelife.com/2024/05/23/flowcopter-successfully-tests-heavylift-cargo-drone-at-hca-airport/)
- **2024-01** - Secured close to £1m from Scottish Enterprise to develop greener heavy-lift industrial drones. [source](https://www.suasnews.com/2024/01/flowcopter-lands-funding-from-scottish-enterprise-to-develop-greener-greater-drones-to-rival-helicopters/)

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/flowcopter

companies.name: Flyby Technology Limited
companies.summary: York-based developer and manufacturer of armed VTOL UAVs for military and government customers, alongside professional drone training.
companies.body:
# Flyby Technology

> York-based developer and manufacturer of armed VTOL UAVs for military and government customers, alongside professional drone training.

## What they do

Flyby Technology designs and manufactures vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) unmanned air systems for military, government and commercial customers. The company's flagship platform is the JACKAL, an all-electric VTOL UAV capable of carrying and firing air-to-surface missiles. Flyby also operates as a Civil Aviation Authority-authorised Recognised Assessment Entity, providing professional beyond-visual-line-of-sight (BVLOS) drone pilot training delivered by former RAF pilots.

## Products and solutions

- **JACKAL** - all-electric VTOL UAV with approximately 80 nautical miles range and up to 65 kg payload capacity; demonstrated firing Thales Lightweight Multirole Missiles (LMMs) from a hover. Flyby holds full UK manufacturing and export rights to the design.
- **Havoc** - larger planned variant with hybrid propulsion for extended range and heavier weapon carriage.
- **Military drone training** - BVLOS pilot training programme written and delivered by ex-RAF qualified weapons instructors, aimed at military and government operators.
- **Military consultancy and design** - bespoke UAV design and capability development for defence customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-12** - Awarded UK MoD contract to supply JACKAL UAVs for a force-protection demonstration of autonomous battlefield systems (Internet of Battlespace Things), with initial delivery before 31 March 2026. [source](https://www.flightglobal.com/military-uavs/flyby-scores-uk-mod-contract-for-airborne-effectors-as-part-of-force-protection-demonstration/165502.article)
- **2023-09** - Taiwan signed a contract for 160 JACKAL drones, the platform's first export order. [source](https://www.flightglobal.com/defence/jackal-armed-uav-scores-first-order-with-160-unit-taiwan-deal/154979.article)

## Links

- Website: https://www.flybytechnology.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/flyby-technology

companies.name: FNH UK Limited
companies.summary: The UK's only onshore manufacturer of military rifles, machine guns and weapon mounts, supplying the UK Ministry of Defence and law enforcement agencies.
companies.body:
# FN UK

> The UK's only onshore manufacturer of military rifles, machine guns and weapon mounts, supplying the UK Ministry of Defence and law enforcement agencies.

## What they do

FNH UK Limited (trading as FN UK) is the UK-based subsidiary of FN Herstal, part of the FN Browning Group, owned by the Wallonia regional government of Belgium. Operating from a facility in Erith, Kent, FN UK manufactures small-calibre weapons and weapon mounts under licence for the UK MoD and UK law enforcement customers. The company was formerly Manroy Engineering, acquired by FN Herstal in 2017. FN UK holds an in-house design and engineering team and CNC, paint, chrome finishing, and phosphating capabilities.

## Products and solutions

- **.50 M2/HMG** - heavy machine gun manufactured for UK Armed Forces.
- **GPMG/MAG 58** - general-purpose machine gun manufactured for UK Armed Forces.
- **5.56mm rifles** - military assault rifles manufactured in the UK.
- **Weapon mounts** - vehicle and platform weapon mounts designed and manufactured to UK MoD requirements.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Silver award in the Armed Forces Covenant Employer Recognition Scheme; working towards Gold status.

## Recent news

- **2025** - Celebrating 50 years of UK manufacturing operations, supported by parent FN Herstal's 135-year heritage in small-calibre weapons design.
- **2024** - DE&S awarded FN UK a £20 million contract to upgrade the UK Armed Forces' Heavy Machine Gun, with deliveries staged between March 2026 and March 2028. [source](https://des.mod.uk/des-awards-fn-uk-20m-contract-to-upgrade-uk-armed-forces-heavy-machine-gun/)

## Links

- Website: https://fnherstal.com/en/news/fnh-uk/

companies.name: Forcys Limited
companies.summary: Maritime defence company delivering integrated underwater sensing, communications and payload solutions for navies and defence organisations worldwide.
companies.body:
# Forcys

> Maritime defence company delivering integrated underwater sensing, communications and payload solutions for navies and defence organisations worldwide.

## What they do

Forcys integrates, delivers and supports customer-shaped solutions for underwater and maritime defence operations, drawing on 50 years of combined domain expertise across its constituent businesses. The company brings together technologies covering sonar, optical systems, navigation, communications and command and control into payloads for unmanned underwater vehicles (UUVs), remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) and surface platforms. It serves navies, government defence agencies, and critical national infrastructure protection programmes across the UK, US, Australia and allied nations.

Forcys is part of the Covelya Group. In February 2026, Wavefront Systems formally merged with Chelsea Technologies and Forcys, with all three operating under the Forcys brand.

## Products and solutions

- **Sentinel IDS** - intruder detection sonar capable of detecting, tracking and classifying divers and UUVs at ranges up to 1,000m and 1,500m respectively; described as the world's most deployed underwater intruder detection sonar.
- **Solstice MAS** - multi-aperture sonar for high-resolution seabed mapping on AUV platforms.
- **Vigilant FLS** - forward-looking sonar for obstacle avoidance and navigation.
- **Sentry IMS** - intruder monitoring system for protected area surveillance.
- **Equinox** - remotely operated towed vehicle for mine countermeasures operations.
- **ROV and UUV payloads** - integrated sensor suites for underwater identification, ISR and environmental assessment missions.
- **Secure diver communications** - encrypted acoustic communications for diver and subsurface operations.
- **Tactical modules** - containerised expeditionary solutions for rapid deployment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group
- **Other:** Armed Forces Covenant Bronze Award (Covelya Group)

## Recent news

- **2026-06-17** - Forcys awarded contract to deploy Sentinel IDS across multiple critical national infrastructure sites for an allied nation. [Offshore Source](https://offshoresource.com/news/company-updates/forcys-wins-key-contract-to-protect-multiple-critical-national-infrastructure-sites)
- **2026-02** - Wavefront Systems formally merged into Forcys alongside Chelsea Technologies, consolidating underwater sensing capabilities under a single brand. [Ocean Science & Technology](https://www.oceansciencetechnology.com/news/wavefront-systems-joins-forcys-group-merger-strengthening-underwater-solutions/)
- **2026-02** - Forcys and QinetiQ signed a partnership to develop deployable underwater test ranges and systems in support of Australian maritime assurance. [Naval Technology](https://www.naval-technology.com/news/qinetiq-forcys-australia-maritime/)
- **2026-03** - Kraken Robotics announced it is acquiring Covelya Group, the parent company of Forcys. [Offshore Source](https://offshoresource.com/news/finance/kraken-robotics-to-acquire-covelya-group-limited/)

## Links

- Website: https://www.forcys.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/forcys-ltd

companies.name: Ford Aerospace Limited
companies.summary: Specialist manufacturer of precision components, laminated shims and assemblies for aerospace, defence and high-technology industries.
companies.body:
# Ford Aerospace Limited

> Specialist manufacturer of precision components, laminated shims and assemblies for aerospace, defence and high-technology industries.

## What they do

Ford Aerospace manufactures and supplies precision machined and fabricated components, assemblies, and adjustable laminated shim products to the aerospace and defence supply chain. The company holds approved supplier status with several major defence and aerospace primes including BAE Systems, Leonardo, Airbus, GKN, Moog Aerospace, Rolls-Royce and Safran. Its portfolio covers surface-bonded and edge-bonded parts in metallic and non-metallic materials, with customisable shim products that replace traditional shim sets in complex assembly applications.

Founded in 1910 and incorporated in 1944, the company is part of the SPIROL Group, a global manufacturer of engineered fasteners, disc springs and shims.

## Products and solutions

- **Precision machined components** - make-to-print components for aerospace and defence assemblies.
- **Laminated shim products** - adjustable and peelable surface and edge-bonded shims in metallic and non-metallic materials, in customisable thicknesses and lamination configurations.
- **Fabricated assemblies** - fabricated metal parts and sub-assemblies for high-technology industry customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, AS/EN 9100 (Aviation, Space and Defence Quality Management System)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Make UK Defence, North West Aerospace Alliance, North East Aerospace Alliance

companies.summary: Creative consultancy specialising in brand, digital and motion communications for defence, nuclear, energy and other critical industries.
companies.body:
# Forepoint Limited

> Creative consultancy specialising in brand, digital and motion communications for defence, nuclear, energy and other critical industries.

## What they do

Forepoint is a creative consultancy with over 30 years of experience working with critical industries including defence, nuclear, energy, and safety sectors. The company delivers strategic communications programmes covering brand strategy and development, digital and web, and motion and video production. It works with clients at times of significant change, including major transformation programmes, cultural change initiatives, supply chain communications, and public and stakeholder engagement. Forepoint holds Cyber Essentials and Cyber Essentials Plus certification.

Defence clients include BAE Systems, with whom Forepoint has worked since 2006 as one of eight agencies on BAE Systems' design roster, supporting air, land and sea divisions. The company also works with the Dreadnought Alliance, AWE, and the Nuclear Decommissioning Authority.

## Products and solutions

- **Brand** - brand strategy, brand development, communications, design services and exhibitions for defence and critical industry clients.
- **Digital** - digital strategy, website development, hosting and maintenance, app development and social media.
- **Motion** - video production, 2D and 3D animation, scripting and immersive content for mission-critical communications.

companies.name: Form1 Partners Ltd
companies.summary: Technology commercialisation partner connecting defence and security scale-ups with established corporates and primes.
companies.body:
# Form1 Partners Ltd

> Technology commercialisation partner connecting defence and security scale-ups with established corporates and primes.

## What they do

Form1 Partners is a commercialisation consultancy focused on B2B technology scale-ups and the corporates and primes that seek to work with them. The company has a specific defence and national security practice, staffed by personnel with backgrounds spanning MOD, RAF, Strategic Command, jHub and industry primes. It works with scale-ups to build partnerships with established defence corporates, and with corporates to access and qualify emerging technology from the SME and start-up market.

Form1 was appointed by Leonardo UK as its SME Collaboration Partner to run the Leonardo SME Collaboration Partner Programme, launched in March 2025. The programme identifies and operationalises long-term partnerships between Leonardo's UK business and defence and security SMEs, with the aim of delivering sovereign capability to UK defence and allied forces.

## Products and solutions

- **Corporate partnership advisory** - identifying, structuring and closing technology partnerships between scale-ups and established defence and security corporates.
- **SME programme management** - managing structured SME collaboration programmes on behalf of primes, including sourcing, qualification and relationship management.
- **VC and investor advisory** - supporting venture capital investors with defence sector access and qualification of portfolio company commercial opportunities.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-03** - Leonardo UK launched its SME Collaboration Partner Programme with Form1 Partners as the appointed commercialisation partner; the first partnership under the programme paired Leonardo with Faculty AI. [Army Technology](https://www.army-technology.com/news/leonardos-sme-programme-uk/)

companies.body:
# Fortinet, Inc.

> Cyber security vendor providing network and platform security, with a UK operating company serving public sector and defence.

## What they do

Fortinet is a cyber security company founded in 2000 and headquartered in Sunnyvale, California. It develops firewalls, secure networking, SASE and security operations products, integrated through its Security Fabric platform. The UK operating company, Fortinet UK Limited (Companies House 04570027), serves customers across UK industry and government, including public sector and defence applications.

## Products and solutions

- **FortiGate / FortiOS** - next-generation firewall hardware and operating system. FortiOS 8.0 was announced in 2026 with capabilities for AI security and SASE.
- **Security Fabric** - integrated platform connecting Fortinet's security and networking products.
- **Defence solutions** - products positioned to extend cyber security across the battlespace at the operational level.

companies.name: Foster & Freeman Limited
companies.summary: UK designer and manufacturer of forensic examination equipment for questioned documents, fingerprints, trace evidence, identity verification, and border control.
companies.body:
# Foster + Freeman

> UK designer and manufacturer of forensic examination equipment for questioned documents, fingerprints, trace evidence, identity verification, and border control.

## What they do

Foster + Freeman designs and manufactures systems for the forensic examination of questioned documents, the detection and enhancement of latent fingerprints, and the elemental analysis of trace evidence. The company also produces identity verification and border control technology for travel document authentication. Products are built to order at the company's headquarters in Evesham, Worcestershire, and exported to more than 170 countries. End-users include the Ministry of Defence, law enforcement agencies, border forces, and military organisations worldwide.

## Products and solutions

- **VSC Range (Video Spectral Comparator)** - Workstations for forensic examination of identity, travel, and questioned documents; used at borders and in forensic laboratories.
- **Crime-lite Range** - Forensic alternate light sources for crime scene examination and fingerprint evidence recovery, including handheld and laboratory imaging systems.
- **DCS 6** - Fingermark imaging workstation providing multi-spectral photography for latent print capture and enhancement.
- **VSC ForeFront** - Portable triple-layer identity authentication device for frontline border operations.
- **ffTA (Trace Analysis System)** - Laboratory platform for glass refractive index measurement, Raman spectroscopy, and micro spectrometry of trace evidence.
- **CBRNE and TSCM solutions** - Tactical products for chemical, biological, radiological, nuclear, and explosive environments, and technical surveillance countermeasures.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-04-01** - Launched VSC ForeFront, a triple-layer identity authentication device for frontline border operations. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/fosterfreeman-launches-vsc-forefront-triple-layer-identity-authentication-for-frontline-operations/)
- **2026-03-01** - Announced ContraSCAN detection technology targeting the prison drug detection market. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/fosterfreeman-to-tackle-prison-drug-crisis-with-launch-of-contrascan-detection-technology/)
- **2026-03-01** - Supported the JSaRC Immersive Experience at Security and Policing 2026. [source](https://fosterfreeman.com/security-policing-2026-supporting-the-jsarc-lmmersive-experience/)

## Links

companies.name: FR Aviation Ltd t/a Draken Europe
companies.summary: Provider of adversary air, electronic warfare and operational readiness training services to the UK MoD and NATO allies, operating as FR Aviation Ltd.
companies.body:
# Draken Europe

> Provider of adversary air, electronic warfare and operational readiness training services to the UK MoD and NATO allies, operating as FR Aviation Ltd.

## What they do

Draken Europe is the trading name of FR Aviation Ltd, a UK entity with roots in 1985 when it was established to deliver air defence training for the Royal Navy following the Falklands conflict. FR Aviation became part of Cobham Plc and was acquired by Draken International in 2020, uniting Draken's US-based adversary air expertise with FR Aviation's live electronic warfare training capability. The company operates from Bournemouth Airport (Hurn) and Teesside Airport, delivering mission-critical training services to government and military customers in the UK and more than 25 nations. Draken International is majority-owned by private equity specialist Blackstone.

## Products and solutions

- **Red Force** - an integrated multi-domain adversary training service combining Adversary Air, Electronic Warfare, Surface Threats, Uncrewed Systems and Situational Awareness elements.
- **Sword (Adversary Air)** - contracted aggressor air services using ex-military aircraft including the L-159E Honey Badger and Dassault Falcon 20, replicating enemy tactics for Gen 4 and Gen 5 operators.
- **Shield (Electronic Warfare)** - live electronic attack, threat simulation, communications jamming and silent target capabilities.
- **Sting (Surface Threats)** - surface threat replication including multiple emitters and platforms with representative signatures.
- **Shadow (Uncrewed Systems)** - uncrewed threat replication services addressing the growing demand for drone threat training.
- **Phantom Sky (Situational Awareness)** - digital backbone of the Red Force suite, providing situational awareness data across the training environment.
- **Aircraft MRO and engineering** - maintenance, repair and overhaul services for the company's own fleet and external customers.
- **Search and rescue and surveillance** - specialist aviation services for government customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group.

## Recent news

- **2025-01-29** - Awarded £173 million Interim Medium Speed Operational Readiness Training Services (IMSORTS) contract by the UK MoD, running from January 2025, delivering training for the RAF and Royal Navy from Teesside Airport and Bournemouth. [source](https://aviationweek.com/defense/light-attack-advanced-training/draken-europe-provide-training-uk-royal-air-force-navy)

companies.name: Framatome Limited
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of the French nuclear technology group Framatome, providing engineering, component manufacturing, instrumentation and control, and fuel services to the British nuclear, chemical, process, and defence industries.
companies.body:
# Framatome Ltd

> UK subsidiary of the French nuclear technology group Framatome, providing engineering, component manufacturing, instrumentation and control, and fuel services to the British nuclear, chemical, process, and defence industries.

## What they do

Framatome Limited is the UK entity of Framatome, an international nuclear technology company majority-owned by EDF. The UK business has operated in the British nuclear industry for over 25 years and employs more than 350 people across sites in Avonmouth (Bristol), Cranfield (Bedfordshire), Hinkley Point C, and Sizewell B. The company provides engineering services, computational software code development, project management, site and outage support, instrumentation and control systems, and component manufacturing. Framatome explicitly lists the British Defence industry among its UK customer sectors. The parent group operates in space and healthcare alongside its core nuclear business.

## Products and solutions

- **Nuclear engineering and services** - Design and service engineering, site outage support, and long-term operations support for existing UK nuclear power plants.
- **Instrumentation and control systems** - I&C solutions for nuclear plant safety and operations.
- **Component manufacturing** - High-quality components for nuclear steam supply systems, including post-weld heat treatment services.
- **Computational software code development** - Software tools for nuclear engineering analysis.
- **Nuclear fuel services** - Fuel design and manufacturing capability; Framatome has announced plans to establish a nuclear fuel fabrication facility in the UK.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Nuclear Industry Association

## Recent news

- **2025-11-01** - Signed an agreement with NNB for post weld heat treatments on Hinkley Point C main secondary coolant system welds. [source](https://www.framatome.com/medias/framatome-signs-an-agreement-with-nnb-for-post-weld-heat-treatments-on-the-welds-of-the-main-secondary-coolant-systems-of-hinkley-point-c/?lang=en)
- **2025-01-01** - Partnered with Perpetual Atomics to collaborate on deep space nuclear power sources. [source](https://www.aero-mag.com/framatome-and-perpetual-atomics-collaborate-on-deep-space-nuclear-power-sources)

## Links

- Website: https://www.framatome.com/en/about/locations/united-kingdom/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/framatome-ltd

Public information verified 2026-06-29. companies.name: Francis Kirk (Socket Screws) Limited
companies.summary: Manchester-based distributor and stockist of specialist fasteners, socket products, PPE, and industrial supplies, serving the aerospace and other precision engineering sectors.
companies.body:
# Francis Kirk (Socket Screws) Ltd

> Manchester-based distributor and stockist of specialist fasteners, socket products, PPE, and industrial supplies, serving the aerospace and other precision engineering sectors.

## What they do

Francis Kirk is a fifth-generation family business founded in 1868, operating from a 24,000 square foot warehouse and trade counter in Denton, Manchester. The company supplies fasteners, socket screws, bolts, nuts, washers, hand tools, PPE, and industrial consumables. It holds exclusive UK distribution rights for the Philidas self-locking nut range, and is the only UK stockist to carry Philidas, Unbrako, and Holo-Krome fasteners under one roof. Francis Kirk is a member of the North West Aerospace Alliance and supplies the aerospace sector; its ADS Group membership confirms its position within the aerospace, defence, and security supply chain.

## Products and solutions

- **Philidas self-locking nuts** - Anti-vibration all-metal locking nuts used in aerospace, automotive, rail, renewable energy, and defence applications.
- **Socket screws and fasteners** - Socket head caps (DIN 912), countersunk, button, and set screw ranges in metric and imperial, including Unbrako and Holo-Krome branded products.
- **Nord-Lock washers** - Wedge-locking washers for high-vibration environments.
- **PPE and workwear** - Industrial personal protective equipment, high-visibility clothing, and footwear.
- **Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) and Kanban** - Inventory management services for industrial customers.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** ISO 9001, ISO 14001
- **Memberships:** ADS Group, North West Aerospace Alliance, British and Irish Association of Fastener Distributors (BIAFD)

## Links

- Website: https://www.franciskirk.com
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/francis-kirk

## Sources

Public information verified 2026-06-29. companies.name: Frankenburg Technologies Ltd
companies.summary: Estonian defence technology startup building affordable, mass-manufacturable guided interceptor missile systems for counter-UAS and short-range air defence, with a UK registered entity and investment in UK R&D.
companies.body:
# Frankenburg Technologies Ltd

> Estonian defence technology startup building affordable, mass-manufacturable guided interceptor missile systems for counter-UAS and short-range air defence, with a UK registered entity and investment in UK R&D.

## What they do

Frankenburg Technologies is a European defence technology company founded in 2024 and headquartered in Tallinn, Estonia, with a UK entity registered in London. The company designs and manufactures low-cost guided interceptor missiles intended for mass production and rapid replenishment, addressing what it describes as a cost and scale gap in modern air defence. Frankenburg's Mark I missile is designed to intercept Class III UAS targets and other aerial threats. The company has operations in Estonia, Latvia (including a missile assembly site in Riga), Lithuania, the UK, and Ukraine, and has committed to investing in UK research and development. Partners include Babcock, Airbus, Milrem Robotics, and Colt CZ Group. The UK entity's SIC codes include defence activities (84220).

## Products and solutions

- **Mark I Guided Interceptor Missile** - The company's first product; described as the smallest and lowest-cost guided interceptor missile to have completed a live-fire intercept. Engineered for mass production using commercially available components and integrated across land, air, and maritime platforms.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-06-23** - Opened Riga missile assembly site, described as the first affordable mass-production missile factory of its kind. [source](https://frankenburg.tech/frankenburg-technologies-opens-riga-missile-assembly-site-as-first-affordable-mass-production-missile-factory-in-the-world/)
- **2026-06-16** - Partnered with Milrem Robotics on an unmanned mobile counter-UAS missile defence capability. [source](https://frankenburg.tech/frankenburg-technologies-and-milrem-robotics-partner-on-unmanned-mobile-counter-uas-missile-defence-capability/)
- **2026-05-27** - Partnered with Colt CZ Group on counter-UAS missile systems for Czechia and Central and Eastern Europe. [source](https://frankenburg.tech/frankenburg-technologies-and-colt-cz-group-partner-on-counter-uas-missile-systems-for-czechia-and-central-and-eastern-europe/)
- **2026-03-30** - Completed a live demonstration with Airbus integrating the Mark I with the Bird of Prey uncrewed interceptor. [source](https://frankenburg.tech/successful-first-demo-flight-airbus-uncrewed-bird-of-prey-interceptor-autonomously-engages-kamikaze-drone-with-frankenburg-missile/)
- **2025-01-01** - Raised EUR 30 million to build European missile manufacturing capacity. [source](https://frankenburg.tech/frankenburg-technologies-raises-30m-to-build-europes-next-generation-missile-manufacturing-capacity/)

## Links

- Website: https://frankenburg.tech
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/frankenburg-tech

## Sources

companies.name: Fraunhofer UK Research Ltd
companies.summary: UK affiliate of the Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft, providing applied photonics research and development to industry across security, space, sensing and quantum technologies.
companies.body:
# Fraunhofer UK Research Ltd

> UK affiliate of the Fraunhofer-Gesellschaft, providing applied photonics research and development to industry across security, space, sensing and quantum technologies.

## What they do

Fraunhofer UK Research Ltd is a not-for-profit company limited by guarantee, established in 2012 in partnership with the University of Strathclyde. It oversees Fraunhofer Research Centres in the UK and provides a central UK point of contact for the wider Fraunhofer global network. Its principal research activity is the Centre for Applied Photonics (Fraunhofer CAP), which performs industry-driven laser and optical systems research for sectors including security and defence, space, healthcare and renewable energy.

## Products and solutions

- **Applied photonics R&D** - industry-contracted research in laser and optical technologies, with applications in security, environmental monitoring, sensing and quantum technologies.
- **Fraunhofer CAP** - the first UK Fraunhofer Centre, based at the University of Strathclyde's Technology and Innovation Centre; focuses on enabling new or improved products and processes for industrial partners.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.body:
# Frazer-Nash Consultancy

> Systems, engineering and technology consultancy serving UK defence; part of the KBR group.

## What they do

Frazer-Nash Consultancy is a UK systems, engineering and technology company that delivers engineering and technology solutions across defence, nuclear, transport, energy and other sectors. In defence it provides systems engineering, procurement support, design assurance and through-life support, with a long-established practice in submarine and surface-ship work. The company operates from a network of UK and Australian offices and is part of the US-headquartered KBR group, which acquired it in 2021.

## Products and solutions

- **Systems engineering and assurance** - design, integration and safety assurance for defence platforms including submarines and surface ships.
- **Procurement and acquisition support** - technical advisory and programme support to defence customers.
- **Technology and digital services** - modelling, simulation, data and digital engineering for complex systems.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group; Society of Maritime Industries.

## Recent news

- **2025** - Part of Team Calibre (with KBR and V.Group) supporting the Royal Navy's Type 31 frigate through-life support, expected to commence Q4 2025. [source](https://www.fnc.co.uk/discover-frazer-nash/news/kbr-frazer-nash-and-vgroup-combine-expertise-to-strengthen-uk-naval-capability/)

companies.summary: Specialist logistics provider serving the aerospace and defence sectors, offering time-critical road and air freight, customs clearance and warehousing across Europe.
companies.body:
# Freightline Carriers Ltd

> Specialist logistics provider serving the aerospace and defence sectors, offering time-critical road and air freight, customs clearance and warehousing across Europe.

## What they do

Freightline Carriers is a UK-based logistics specialist with more than 50 years of heritage in freight forwarding. The company operates 24/7 with a multilingual team and access to over 1,000 vehicles across Europe. It holds a dedicated aerospace and defence practice focused on transporting sensitive, high-value components and equipment, and supporting manufacturing, maintenance, repair and overhaul supply chains.

## Products and solutions

- **Urgent, time-critical logistics** - 24/7 road and air freight with 15-minute response and two-hour collection commitment; used in aerospace and defence supply chains to prevent production line stoppages or aircraft groundings.
- **Air freight and air charter** - global transport of aerospace and defence cargo including helicopters, private jets and large payload aircraft.
- **Customs clearance** - in-house agents handling cross-border documentation for defence materiel.
- **Warehousing and distribution** - European network of storage facilities with integrated warehouse management systems for high-value stock.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.name: Frequentis (UK) Limited
companies.summary: UK subsidiary of Frequentis AG, an Austrian-listed supplier of communication and information systems for safety-critical control centres, with a dedicated defence portfolio covering military ATC, secure voice, C2 and networks.
companies.body:
# Frequentis (UK) Limited

> UK subsidiary of Frequentis AG, an Austrian-listed supplier of communication and information systems for safety-critical control centres, with a dedicated defence portfolio covering military ATC, secure voice, C2 and networks.

## What they do

Frequentis AG is headquartered in Vienna and listed on the Vienna and Frankfurt stock exchanges. It develops communication and information systems for safety-critical environments, serving air traffic management, defence, public safety, maritime and public transport sectors. The UK entity, Frequentis (UK) Limited, is registered in Twickenham. The Group employs more than 2,600 people and operates in over 50 countries. Frequentis is a long-standing supplier to UK air navigation service provider NATS, and has assumed delivery responsibility for the NATS Main Voice System and Arrivals Manager System as part of the Deploying SESAR project.

## Products and solutions

- **Military air traffic control (MilATC)** - modular ATC systems for military heliports, transport hubs, fighter, naval and joint-use airbases; includes tower automation integrating weather, flight data processing, surveillance and airbase lighting.
- **Secure voice** - communication systems for military and government control centres requiring security-accredited voice over IP and radio integration.
- **Remote digital tower** - replaces out-of-window tower views with a visualisation system; applicable to military ATC where physical tower presence is impractical.
- **Command and control** - information and communications systems enabling coordinated decision-making in air defence and multi-domain operations.
- **Networks** - mission-critical communications networks for defence and air defence applications.
- **Recording** - voice and data recording for safety-critical and evidential use.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-05-28** - Frequentis and NATS representatives presented jointly at Airspace World 2026 in Lisbon on SESAR sustainability initiatives. [source](https://www.eurocontrol.int/sites/default/files/2026-05/airspace-world-2026-full-programme.pdf)
- **2021-08-12** - Frequentis acquired the ATC Solutions business unit of L3Harris Technologies, taking on the Main Voice System and Arrivals Manager System for NATS. [source](https://www.frequentis.com/en/pr/frequentis-strengthens-position-strategic-partner-uk-air-navigation-service-provider-nats)

## Links

- Website: https://www.frequentis.com/en/defence
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/frequentis

## Sources

Sources: Frequentis corporate website (frequentis.com/en/defence), Companies House (find-and-update.company-information.service.gov.uk/company/02704890), Frequentis press release on NATS partnership (frequentis.com/en/pr/frequentis-strengthens-position-strategic-partner-uk-air-navigation-service-provider-nats), Airspace World 2026 programme (eurocontrol.int), Wikipedia (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequentis).

companies.summary: Engineering design, analysis and consultancy firm serving aerospace, defence, marine, nuclear and space sectors, with accredited UK security clearance and Cyber Essentials Plus certification.
companies.body:
# Frewer & Co. Engineers Limited

> Engineering design, analysis and consultancy firm serving aerospace, defence, marine, nuclear and space sectors, with accredited UK security clearance and Cyber Essentials Plus certification.

## What they do

Frewer Engineering, trading name of Frewer & Co. Engineers Limited, was founded in 2003 and is based in Ockley, Surrey. The company provides engineering design and analysis consultancy services across demanding sectors including aerospace, defence, gas turbines, marine, nuclear and space. Its defence work spans land-based military equipment, airborne propulsion systems, ballistic protection and fluid dynamics for military platforms. The team holds full UK security clearance and the company holds Cyber Essentials Plus accreditation. It is registered on JOSCAR and is a member of Make UK Defence.

## Products and solutions

- **Structural analysis for military platforms** - design and structural assurance of land vehicles, weapons components and airborne structures, including lightweight metallic and composite structures.
- **Ballistic protection analysis** - ballistic modelling and light-weighting for composite body armour and helmet systems.
- **Fluid flow analysis** - aerodynamic and hydrodynamic performance optimisation and flow-induced loading for military equipment.
- **Gas turbine and propulsion engineering** - design and analysis of military aircraft propulsion systems.
- **Composites design** - carbon composite structures for aerospace and defence applications.
- **Rotor dynamics** - engineering analysis for rotating systems across defence and aerospace programmes.
- **UAS programme support** - engineering support to unmanned aerial systems development (referenced in January 2026 news).

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Frameworks:** JOSCAR
- **Accreditations:** Cyber Essentials Plus, ISO 9001 (BSI certified)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group, Make UK Defence, Composites UK, NAFEMS, IMechE

## Recent news

- **2026-05-29** - May 2026 newsletter highlighted composites expertise, BSI reaccreditation and CFD assessment work. [source](https://frewer-engineering.com/2026/05/29/latest-news-may-2026/)
- **2026-04-30** - April 2026 newsletter reported a visit from Make UK Defence and Frewer joining the D3IP programme. [source](https://frewer-engineering.com/2026/04/30/latest-news-april-2026/)

companies.summary: Geo-data solutions company providing subsea survey, maritime domain awareness and critical underwater infrastructure monitoring services to government and defence clients.
companies.body:
# Fugro GB Limited

> Geo-data solutions company providing subsea survey, maritime domain awareness and critical underwater infrastructure monitoring services to government and defence clients.

## What they do

Fugro is a Dutch-listed global geo-data company with a long-established UK entity (Fugro GB Limited, Wallingford, Oxfordshire, incorporated 1973). The UK operation delivers geophysical, hydrographic and geotechnical survey services and supports subsea domain awareness for national security and critical infrastructure protection. Fugro deploys a fleet of uncrewed surface vessels, subsea robotics and remote operations centres to deliver persistent maritime surveillance and data collection.

The parent company, Fugro NV, is headquartered in Leidschendam, Netherlands, and listed on Euronext Amsterdam. Fugro GB Limited is the primary UK legal entity and ADS member; separate UK entities include Fugro GB (North) Marine Limited (Edinburgh) and Fugro Subsea Services Ltd.

## Products and solutions

- **Uncrewed Surface Vessels (USVs)** - persistent, low-risk maritime surveillance and geophysical data acquisition in complex operational zones.
- **Subsea Robotics and Remote Operations** - inspection, repair and maintenance of critical underwater infrastructure including cables and pipelines.
- **Hydrographic and Geophysical Survey** - data acquisition supporting maritime situational awareness and infrastructure resilience for government and defence clients.
- **Subsea Domain Awareness Platform** - data fusion platform (developed with partners DTACT and Ubotica) combining geo-data, satellite intelligence and other data streams for detection of threats to subsea infrastructure.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-07-01** - Fugro announced a partnership with DTACT and Ubotica to develop a subsea domain awareness and critical infrastructure monitoring platform, initially targeting government organisations in the North Sea and Baltic regions. [source](https://www.marinetechnologynews.com/news/fugro-finds-partners-subsea-650778)
- **2025-04-18** - Fugro and Damen announced a joint venture to provide the Royal Netherlands Navy with a marine security and surveillance vessel and operating crew under a two-year charter awarded by the Dutch Ministry of Defence. [source](https://www.navalnews.com/naval-news/2025/04/fugro-and-damen-to-support-the-royal-netherlands-navy-with-marine-security-and-surveillance-vessel/)

## Links

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/fugro

companies.body:
# Fulcrum Defence

> Veteran-owned UK supplier of bespoke equipment carriage, packaging and protection solutions for defence and emergency services.

## What they do

Fulcrum Defence is a veteran-owned UK firm that project-manages the design and delivery of bespoke equipment carriage, packaging and protection solutions for the military and emergency services. Its focus is protecting and transporting sensitive or mission-critical equipment in demanding operational environments, with attention to ergonomics and rapid access. It works through a network of partners and suppliers to deliver custom solutions to defence standards, including codification and NSN compliance.

## Products and solutions

- **Communications and walk-on fit cases** - carriage systems for communications equipment.

# Futronics Limited

> Designer and manufacturer of vehicle-based command and control, mobile data, communications and ANPR enforcement systems for policing and law enforcement.

## What they do

Futronics designs and manufactures vehicle-mounted command and control systems, mobile data terminals, ANPR (Automatic Number Plate Recognition) enforcement systems, optical fingerprint scanners and video monitoring systems. The company serves police forces worldwide and has been a technology partner to the UK Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) for its Home Office approved ANPR enforcement capability. Products are deployed across emergency services, transport and public safety organisations.

- **eFP / eFR** - optical fingerprint scanner and fingerprint reader units for law enforcement.

# Futurum Consultancy and Training Ltd

> Specialist intelligence, surveillance, and covert tradecraft training and consultancy for defence, law enforcement, and government clients.

## What they do

Futurum provides discreet, practitioner-led training and consultancy to defence, law enforcement, and government clients internationally. The company has served clients in over 50 countries, with expertise spanning intelligence analysis, covert tradecraft, countering corporate espionage, counter-terrorism, and crisis management. # GKM Aerospace

> Precision CNC machining and turnkey component manufacture for the aerospace and defence sectors.

## What they do

G.K.M. (Aerospace) Limited is a precision engineering manufacturer based in Basildon, Essex, supplying finished components and assemblies to leading OEMs and tiered suppliers in the aerospace, defence, and communications industries. The company operates a multi-shift CNC machining facility and works from engineering drawings and 3D models to deliver complex metallic and composite parts. # G3 Systems

## What they do

G3 Systems designs and produces deployable infrastructure solutions and delivers engineering, service and support to defence and government customers operating in austere locations. Its work spans deployable and fixed infrastructure and systems, outsourced fire and rescue services, and through-life support. The company is a wholly owned subsidiary of US-headquartered Intrepid Global Solutions and operates from Portland, England.

# GaardTech

> Australian maker of robotic enemy-vehicle targets and live-fire training systems for armed forces.

## What they do

GaardTech designs and manufactures robotic enemy-vehicle targets and live-fire training systems for armies. Its catalogue of 3D Robotic Enemy Vehicles replicates a range of enemy platforms with thermal, electronic and radar signatures, and the mobile targets can be operated remotely or autonomously across terrain. The company is based in Brisbane, Queensland, and exports to several allied armed forces.

## Products and solutions

- **3D Robotic Enemy Vehicles** - mobile robotic targets replicating tanks, armoured personnel carriers and other platforms with multi-spectral signatures for live-fire training.
- **Flatpack robotic targets** - deployable, recyclable robotic targets and decoys.
- **Doctrinally correct enemy formations** - target groupings representing enemy battle groups for collective live-fire training.

companies.name: Galaxkey Limited
companies.summary: Identity-based data protection platform providing encryption for email, files, messaging, and collaboration, with a specific offering for the defence and Official-Sensitive supply chain.
companies.body:
# Galaxkey Limited

> Identity-based data protection platform providing encryption for email, files, messaging, and collaboration, with a specific offering for the defence and Official-Sensitive supply chain.

## What they do

Galaxkey provides a post-quantum ready data protection platform built on a three-layer, identity-based encryption architecture. Organisations retain full control of their own encryption keys. The platform is listed by the NCSC and has a dedicated product configuration for the UK defence and Official-Sensitive supply chain, protecting data that must be shared across contractors and subcontractors beyond secure network boundaries. Clients include KNDS UK (formerly WFEL), the Ministry of Justice, Bradford City Council, UCL, and international financial institutions.

## Products and solutions

- **Secure Emails** - end-to-end encryption for business communications, preventing unauthorised forwarding or sharing.
- **Secure Workspace** - controlled environment for secure collaboration and large file transfer between organisations including defence subcontractors.
- **Secure Files and Digital Rights Management** - persistent protection applied to Official-Sensitive documents regardless of location or recipient system.
- **LockChat** - enterprise secure messaging with identity-based encryption, positioned as a replacement for consumer apps such as WhatsApp in regulated environments.
- **Secure Signatures** - digitally signed contractual and compliance documentation.
- **Secure Shield** - centralised data protection dashboard with e-discovery capability.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** NCSC listed; Cyber Essentials (evidenced on website)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2026-04-20** - Chairman Admiral Sir George Zambellas published commentary on data vulnerability and national security. [source](https://www.galaxkey.com/mythos-vulnerability-and-the-responsibility-we-cannot-ignore-admiral-sir-george-zambellas/)
- **2026-03-05** - Admiral Sir George Zambellas published analysis on data privacy in the context of conflict. [source](https://www.galaxkey.com/privacy-in-data-war-shows-the-weaknesses-admiral-sir-george-zambellas/)

companies.body:
# Gallagher Security Europe Limited

> Physical and cyber security systems manufacturer providing high-security access control, perimeter protection, and NPSA-compliant products to government, defence, and critical national infrastructure.

## What they do

Gallagher designs, manufactures, and supplies security systems covering access control, perimeter protection, intruder alarms, and integrated cybersecurity. The company's UK high-security team delivers solutions to Five Eyes government and critical national infrastructure sites. The High Security Controller 7000 holds compliance awards from the UK National Protective Security Authority (NPSA) under both the Automatic Access Control Systems (AACS) and Cyber Assurance of Physical Security Systems (CAPSS) standards. The UK European headquarters is in Warwick; global headquarters is in Hamilton, New Zealand.

## Products and solutions

- **High Security Controller 7000** - NPSA and CAPSS compliant access control hardware with FIPS-approved microprocessor, authenticated and encrypted reader communications, designed for UK government and Five Eyes high-security sites.
- **Command Centre** - integrated security management software platform covering access control, perimeter, video, and alarm management.
- **Perimeter Security Hardware** - fence controllers, gate hardware, and sensors for critical infrastructure and defence site perimeter protection.
- **PIV Software** - identity credential management for high-security government environments.
- **AccessNow and OneLink** - cloud and networked access control solutions for scalable deployment.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** NPSA AACS compliance; NPSA CAPSS compliance (Controller 7000)
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/gallagher-security

companies.body:
# Galleon Embedded Computing Ltd

> Designer and manufacturer of rugged data recorders, mission computers, and network-attached storage for aerospace and defence platforms.

## What they do

Galleon Embedded Computing develops high-performance, ruggedised data recording and storage solutions for military and commercial aerospace applications. Products span airborne, ground vehicle, and remotely operated vehicle platforms. The company became part of the Spectra Aerospace and Defense group in August 2021 and now trades under the Spectra brand, with the UK registered entity (Companies House 09962683) providing a UK presence.

## Products and solutions

- **Rugged data recorders** - IRIG-106 compliant recorders supporting high-speed multi-sensor data capture, with removable storage up to 64TB.
- **Mission computers and servers** - VPX and XSR-series systems including the XSR Tactical Secure Server, meeting MIL-STD-810, MIL-STD-704, MIL-STD-461 and DO-160 standards.
- **Network-attached storage (NAS)** - High-capacity NAS devices for deployed military environments across land, sea, air, and space domains.
- **C5ISR integrated systems** - End-to-end data capture, processing, storage, and display solutions built to work across platforms without separate integration risk.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Memberships:** ADS Group

companies.body:
# Galvion UK Ltd

> Designer and manufacturer of ballistic helmet systems, soldier power and data management solutions, and future dismounted soldier technologies.

## What they do

Galvion designs and delivers integrated protective head systems and intelligent power and data solutions for military and tactical teams. The company holds a significant position on the UK MOD VIRTUS programme, having supplied over 200,000 helmet systems since contract award in 2015. Galvion's UK entity (Companies House 09713398) supports its British military customer base, with manufacturing for UK and European orders fulfilled in partnership with NP Aerospace. The parent company is headquartered in Montreal, Canada.

## Products and solutions

- **Batlskin helmet systems** - Modular, ballistic protective helmets including the Caiman, Hellbender, and Caiman TL variants, used by UK, US, Canadian, and NATO forces.
- **Nerv Centr power and data systems** - Soldier-worn power management, battery charging, and data distribution systems including the Squad Power Manager (SPM) and MAX-8 Mission Adaptive Charging Station.
- **CORTEX smart helmet integration platform** - Digitally enabled headborne integration system stemming from UK DSTL Future Integrated Dismounted Soldier Vision (FIDSV) programme work.
- **E.D.G headborne power and data system** - Headborne power and data distribution and management for the integrated soldier.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

companies.body:
# GardaWorld

> Private security and risk management company providing government, diplomatic, and defence security services in complex and high-threat environments.

## What they do

GardaWorld is the largest privately-owned security company in the world, headquartered in Montreal, Canada. Its International Protective Services division operates in complex, high-threat, and emerging markets, delivering physical security, close protection, risk analysis, crisis response, and training services to diplomatic, defence, development, and infrastructure clients. In the UK, GardaWorld operates under the entity GW Consulting UK Limited and is a founding signatory of the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Providers.

## Products and solutions

- **Government and defence security services** - Physical security for embassies and consulates, mobile security teams, risk analysis reporting, and emergency planning for MOD and allied-nation government clients.
- **International protective services** - Close protection teams drawing on former US and UK Special Forces personnel, operating in high-threat environments.
- **Counter-terrorism and intelligence services** - Threat assessment, intelligence reporting, and advisory services.
- **Crisis response** - Emergency planning, incident management, and evacuation support.

# Gardiner & Theobald LLP

> Independent construction and property consultancy delivering cost, project, and programme management services to defence, national security, and space clients.

## What they do

Gardiner and Theobald is an independent global consultancy providing cost management, project management, and construction management services across the built environment. Its Defence and National Security practice supports MOD, the Defence Infrastructure Organisation (DIO), prime contractors, and private operators on programmes spanning land, sea, air, cyber, and space domains. Security-cleared teams manage programmes from £100,000 to £100 billion in value. The firm is regulated by RICS and has signed the Armed Forces Covenant.

- **Satellite and space launch programme support** - Cost control, risk management, and regulatory readiness for LEO, MEO, and GEO satellite launch programmes and UK spaceport development.
- **Construction cost management** - Quantity surveying and cost advisory for defence facilities including Royal Navy, British Army, and Royal Air Force estate.

companies.body:
# Gardner Aerospace

> UK manufacturer of precision metallic aerospace components and assemblies for civil and defence aerospace programmes.

## What they do

Gardner Aerospace manufactures machined parts, sheet metal fabrications, and sub-assemblies for the aerospace industry, including defence programmes. The company holds recognised industry approvals and manufactures to AS9100 and NADCAP quality standards. It operates multiple UK facilities, with its principal site in Derby, and has expanded operations internationally including a facility in Bengaluru, India.

The company is owned by Ligeance Aerospace Technology Co. Ltd, a Shenzhen-listed Chinese entity. A 2022 proposed increase in indirect Chinese state investment (via Sichuan Development Holding Co. Ltd) was called in by the UK Government under national security legislation. The acquisition was permitted subject to conditions, including the appointment of an HM Government Observer to the board of the UK subsidiary and the removal of Chinese board members from that entity.

## Products and solutions

- **Machining** - 5-axis CNC milling and high-precision CNC mill/turn of complex metallic aerospace parts.
- **Fabrications** - Precision sheet metal fabrication for aerospace structural and system components.
- **Assembly** - Sub-assembly and full assembly work combining machined, fabricated and welded parts.
- **Speed Shop** - Rapid-turnaround machining capability for urgent aerospace component requirements.

## Frameworks, accreditations and memberships

- **Accreditations:** AS9100, NADCAP
- **Memberships:** ADS Group

## Recent news

- **2025-06-01** - Gardner Aerospace signed an expanded agreement with FDH Aero to support the phased transfer of commercial aircraft programmes as Gardner prepared to open its new facility in Bengaluru. [FDH Aero](https://fdhaero.com/press-release/fdh-aero-signs-expanded-agreement-with-gardner-aerospace/)
